title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On December 26th, Ellery Queen and his father, Inspector Quin, drive a secondhand Duesenberg from a police meeting in Chicago toward the West Virginia village of Aroo. Ellery is driven by interest in a bizarre crime dubbed the "T-shaped murder." The victim, 46-year-old Andrew Van, the eccentric principal of Aroo, was decapitated and nailed to a T-shaped signpost at a T-shaped crossroads on Christmas morning. The crime is characterized by a recurring T-motif, including a T-shaped mark found on the door of Van's house.

At the crime scene, Ellery observes the gruesome details of the execution: four-inch nails were driven through Van's palms, armpits, and ankles to fix his body to the six-foot-tall signpost. The signpost is covered in dried blood. While the local police are baffled, Hampshire County District Attorney Crummett suspects a maniacal killer, noting that Van's only friend, a servant named Clinton, has also disappeared.

Despite Inspector Quin's frustration and desire to return to New York, Ellery is invigorated by the case. They drive into the dreary residential area of Aroo, passing a general store and a car repair shop. Ellery stops the car in front of the Arroyo Village Hall, a shoddy wooden structure, to locate the local sheriff. They find the official, identified by a sign as the Town Marshal, asleep at a desk inside the building.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quin and his father, Inspector Richard Quin of the New York Police Department's Homicide Division, visit Sheriff Redden in the town of Weldon, West Virginia, to investigate the death of Andrew Van. Sheriff Redden explains that on Christmas morning, a farmer named Mike O'Keefe and a man known as Old Pete discovered Van's body hanging from a signpost at a crossroads. The Sheriff, along with the mayor, Matt Hollis, and four others, inspected the body and identified it.

The investigation revealed that Van's house showed signs of a violent struggle, with bloodied furniture and a large "T" painted in blood on the door. Van's servant, Clark, has disappeared, and an axe belonging to Clark—which bore no fingerprints—was found at the scene. The victim's head was missing, leading the Sheriff to speculate the killer took it as a souvenir. The body was sent to the coroner in Wilton County, and the state police and the Hanover County district attorney are now involved.

Following the meeting, Ellery and the Inspector depart by train, though Ellery returns to Weldon on Tuesday. He attempts to obtain information from District Attorney Crummett, but the ambitious official refuses to see him. As Ellery paces the streets, he observes a town gripped by fear and suppressed excitement; while the town celebrates the holidays, the men discuss potential lynching, though no suspect has been identified.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen investigates the death of Andrew Van, a reclusive school principal in Arroyo who was known as a teetotaler and a model teacher. Despite local rumors of his ungodliness spread by Weldon newspapers, Ellery finds little information about Van's past. During an autopsy at the county courthouse, Coroner Stapleton reveals that Van's body has been stored for eight days and is headless. Ellery observes the victim's belongings, including a wallet, a watch, and a letter from a Pittsburgh bookstore, noting that some items bear the initials "AV."

During the proceedings, farmer Michael O'Keefe testifies that he and "Old Pete" discovered Van's body nailed by the hands and feet to a signpost near Arroyo Peak. Old Pete, a disheveled man claiming to have been dead for twenty years, is clarified by Matt Hollis to be a local eccentric who lives in a small house near Arroyo. Mayor Hollis and Sheriff Rudd testify regarding the discovery of the body and a T-shaped mark found on a door at the Fanns' residence.

Luther Bernheim, the local grocer, testifies that while Van was a regular customer who paid in cash, he was never truly friendly. Bernheim notes that Van had unusual tastes, specifically ordering expensive caviar. This detail leads Ellery to suspect that Van's past was more luxurious than his current life suggested. Finally, District Attorney Crummett testifies that Van arrived in Arroyo nine years prior to take the teaching position, living on Arroyo Street with a servant named Klein.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During a legal proceeding regarding the death of Andrew Vance, the district attorney, Krümmit, reveals that Vance was an American citizen who had previously lived in Pittsburgh, where his records identified him as an Armenian born in 1885. Elijah considers the implications of Vance's Armenian background. The prosecution also discusses Clinton, Vance's servant, who was an orphan raised at St. Vincent's Orphanage in Pittsburgh. Krümmit describes Clinton as a simple-minded, gentle, and respectful man. This is supported by the grocer, Bernheim, who describes Clinton as good-natured and exceptionally strong, capable of lifting sugar barrels.

Mayor and banker Matt Hollis testifies that Vance was a hardworking, quiet man who lived in a house rented from the Mayor. Hollis notes that Vance requested strange philosophical and historical books from Pittsburgh and kept his salary in cash at home rather than in the bank. The sheriff testifies that a search of Vance's house on December 25th revealed no money, and the marshal presents an empty, deformed green tin box used by Vance to store his funds. The postmaster testifies that Vance received very little mail and had not sent any letters in several months.

Dr. Strong, the pathologist, testifies that Vance likely died around midnight on Christmas Eve from a blow to the head or a gunshot wound. He notes that the body had nail holes in the hands and feet and that the head was severed with a very sharp tool after death. The coroner, Stapleton, presents a long-handled axe found in Vance's kitchen. The weapon, which belonged to the deceased and was typically used by the missing Clark, bore no fingerprints, suggesting the killer wore gloves or cleaned the blade.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During a courtroom proceeding, West Virginia State Police Commissioner and Colonel Pickert report on the investigation regarding the deceased Andrew Van and the missing servant, Clinton. Pickert states that Van was last seen on Thursday, December 24th, visiting Mrs. Rebecca Traub in Arroyo. Clinton was last seen that same afternoon between Arroyo and Pudding, where he purchased a bushel of potatoes from a farmer named Timothy Trainer. The potatoes were later found untouched at Van's house.

Following this report, a tall police officer brings an elderly, disheveled man into the courtroom. The man has weathered skin, unkempt brown hair, and wears muddy shorts and a gray sweater. He carries a staff topped with a crudely carved snake-shaped statue. 

Casper Crocker, an auto repair shop owner from Wilton, Maine, is called to the witness stand. He testifies that at approximately eleven o'clock on Christmas Eve, a man with dark skin and a clean-shaven face approached his shop. The man, who spoke perfect old-fashioned American English, offered Crocker ten dollars to drive him to an intersection with New Kensington-Bridgeton Road in Arroyo. Crocker agrees and drives him there. Upon arrival, Crocker observes the man walking away while limping on his left leg and carrying a shovel.

After Crocker finishes his testimony, the coroner, Mr. Stapleton, calls the elderly man with the snake staff to the stand. The man, who appears to be praying to his staff, identifies himself not by a human name, but as "Harakhti, the god of the midday sun. Ra-Harakti, which means the hawk!" This declaration leaves the courtroom in a state of stunned silence and fear.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During a trial, a man named Harakti (also referred to as Horus or Harleckt) provides erratic testimony. He claims to be a divine healer and the god Horus, citing Egyptian deities such as Nut, Isis, and Osiris. Colonel Pickert explains to the coroner that Harakti is a traveling medicine showman who sells what is likely cod liver oil under the guise of "sunlight." However, Ellery Quine realizes the significance of Harakti's claims after recognizing a Uraeus emblem on the man's hand, which connects the witness to ancient Egypt.

When questioned about a black-skinned, limping man described by a witness, Harakti identifies the man as his "disciple" and "Great Priest of Horus," named Verga Krossak (or Velja Krosac). Colonel Pickert clarifies that Krossak acted as Harakti's business manager and advertising agent. The Colonel notes that Krossak was last seen on Christmas Eve near Holly Bay, several miles from Wilton, around 10 o'clock. Krossak has since disappeared, and authorities have no leads on him or the witness Crocker.

As the trial continues, evidence is presented regarding a decapitated body nailed to a cross. It is revealed that the iron nails used in the crucifixion came from the victim Andrew Van's own kitchen toolbox. After the coroner, Mr. Swipe, begins his address to the jury, Ellery interrupts the proceedings. He claims to have discovered an "ancient fact" older than Christianity that is relevant to the case, prompting curiosity from the jury and the court.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an inquest regarding a mysterious murder, Ellery Quine addresses the coroner, Stapleton, and District Attorney Crimmett. Ellery points out a significant pattern: the killer left T-shaped symbols throughout the crime scene, including a T-shaped crossroads, a road sign, a corpse, and a bloodstain on the victim's door. Ellery suggests the "T" has religious significance, specifically referring to the Tau cross. When a minister in the audience interrupts to claim the symbol is pagan rather than Christian, Ellery explains that the Tau cross is an ancient symbol that is also known as the "Egyptian cross."

Following the inquest, the jury reaches a verdict of "unknown cause of death by one or more persons." Despite the presence of Colonel Pickert, Crimmett, and various journalists, the investigation yields no progress, and the case fades from the newspapers. Ellery returns to New York, privately suspecting that a man named Vilga Crocok, a con artist-style individual, murdered a foreign-born village schoolteacher to fulfill a twisted, manic mission.

Upon his return, Ellery engages in a disagreement with Inspector Quinn, who dismisses Ellery's focus on complex logic, asserting that most crimes can be explained simply. Six months pass as Ellery assists Quinn with various murder cases in New York. The Arroyo murder case, involving the crucifixion of Andrew Van in West Virginia, resurfaces in Ellery's mind. On a Tuesday morning, while Ellery and Quinn are having breakfast, a servant named Juno delivers a telegram addressed to Ellery.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery receives an early morning telegram from his former university professor, Adler (also referred to as Professor Yadley), an ancient history expert. The telegram invites Ellery to investigate a strange murder that occurred across the street from the professor's residence. The victim, a neighbor, was found nailed to a totem pole in the shape of a cross with his head missing.

Ellery drives his Duesenberg to the location, Bradwood, on Long Island. To ensure he can access the crime scene, Ellery’s father had previously contacted Inspector Quinn, who in turn informed Chief Inspector Wöhn of Nassau County and Prosecutor Essam. These connections allow Ellery to bypass a motorcycle policeman and proceed to the estate.

Upon arrival, Ellery meets Professor Yadley, who explains he is renting a Byzantine-style mansion near the crime scene to study the origins of the Atlantis legend. Yadley notes that he contacted Ellery because of the man's interest in previous murder cases. Ellery is then introduced to Prosecutor Essam and Officer Wain.

The group walks through the vast Bradwood estate toward a dense thicket. They discover the crime scene: a nine-foot-tall, weathered wooden pillar decorated with ghost masks and hybrid animals. At the top of the pillar is a carved eagle with its wings spread wide and flat, forming the shape of a capital letter T.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
A decapitated male corpse is found hanging from a totem pole, which Professor Yadley identifies as either a genuine Native American totem or a convincing fake. The victim, identified as millionaire carpet importer Thomas Braid, is a middle-aged man with a large beer belly. His body is bound by the arms and feet to the eagle's wings of the pole, and a bloodstained hole exists just below the eagle's beak where his head should be.

Officer Vaughn explains that the body was discovered at 7:30 AM by Fox, a driver and gardener, while walking near the main house. Jonah Lincoln, the general manager of Braid's company, was present at the scene and maintained control until police arrived. It is noted that Megalas, another associate, is currently at sea.

As the body is lowered for an autopsy by the medical examiner, Dr. Rumson, the district attorney notes that a bloody chair was found in a nearby gazebo, suggesting the killer used it to reach the nine-foot height. Officer Vance presents Ellery with a red wooden chess piece found in the sandy ground near the totem pole. Upon examining the corpse on the grass, Dr. Rumson discovers a circular red spot on Braid's right palm that resembles paint or dye rather than blood. Ellery realizes the spot is an impression left by the chess piece. Detective Vane confirms this by placing the red chess piece on the victim's palm, where it fits the mark perfectly.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
District Attorney Isaacs informs Ellery and Vane that the victim, Brad, was holding a chess piece when he was killed, which fell from his hand before he was tied up. Isaacs notes that an unfinished chess game exists in Brad's study. The group moves from the grass, where Dr. Rumsen is examining the corpse, to a small, circular, rustic gazebo near a totem pole. Inside the gazebo, they find a table, two chairs, and a large reddish-brown bloodstain on the floor. Ellery discovers a large capital letter "T" painted in fresh blood on the wooden floor behind the table. Professor Yadley notes the strange combination of Native American, Christian, and phallic symbols present at the scene.

Ellery examines a long-handled stone pipe carved with Neptune and his trident near the bloodstain. Although Officer Vorn states the pipe belongs to Brad because of his fingerprints, Ellery notices the pipe contains unburnt tobacco cut into cubes. Ellery collects a sample of this tobacco in a glassine envelope, theorizing that if the cubed tobacco does not match Brad's cigar box, it will serve as a clue identifying the killer.

Ellery hypothesizes that Brad carried a red chess pawn from the house to the gazebo for a specific reason and was attacked while there. Dr. Rumsen returns to report that Brad has been dead for approximately fourteen hours, suggesting the crime occurred in the dark around 10:00 PM. Rumsen also notes that the killer struck Brad directly on the head and observes a strawberry-shaped birthmark on the victim's right knee. As the group leaves the gazebo, Officer Vorn reminds Ellery that he has information to share.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen points out a pattern of "T" shapes at the crime scene, noting the T-shaped totem pole, the victim's decapitated and outstretched body position, and a T-shaped blood smear. He compares this to a murder in West Virginia six months prior, where a victim was nailed to a T-shaped crossroads sign and a T-shaped blood smear was found nearby. Vane identifies a man named Harakat, a lunatic who runs a nudist camp on Oyster Island.

Ellery realizes that the clues associated with Wylie Crosette are reappearing, suggesting a possible sequel to a previous murder. Adair suggests that the T-shaped motifs and crucifixions link the crime to Harakat, though Ellery notes the existence of a mysterious figure named Croscius. Ellery mentions that the only person who can confirm Croscius is Harakat, who is described as a "sun god" figure. Vance reports that Oyster Island is under surveillance but currently poses no immediate danger.

The group enters a colonial-style mansion to meet the bereaved. They encounter Margaret Brad, a forty-five-year-old woman, her daughter Helen Breda, and Jonah Lincoln. The atmosphere is somber and silent. Eames asks Lincoln to explain the circumstances regarding the tickets from the previous night. Lincoln explains that Tom Brad had called him to say he had obtained Broadway tickets for Mrs. Brad, Helen, and Lincoln, and had instructed Lincoln to take the ladies out while he went home.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an investigation into the disappearance of Tom Brad, Jonah Lincoln provides an account of his activities. Lincoln states that he met Mrs. Brad and Helen Brad for dinner in Longchamps and later took them to the Park Theatre. He claims he returned to his office to work on accounts with an auditor, though he later clarifies that the auditor left at eight o'clock, leaving him alone. Lincoln and the Brad women took the Long Island train and a taxi home, arriving around one o'clock. Lincoln notes they did not notice anything unusual, specifically mentioning they did not look at the path leading to the gazebo because it was dark. Mrs. Brad explains she only discovered Tom was missing in the morning when Fox woke them.

The inquiry shifts to personal histories and connections. Lincoln reveals he has an unnamed sister, Hester, who resides on Oyster Island, where a man named Harleot has established a "Sun Cult" involving nudism. Mrs. Brad discloses that Tom was Romanian and had a previous wife in Europe. The discussion turns to Meagal, a Greek amateur explorer who has been sailing on his steam yacht, the *Helen*, for almost a year. Helen Brad suggests Meagal went to the South Pacific. Lincoln notes that Brad and Meagal have been in the carpet import business together for sixteen years and are financially stable, suggesting money is not the motive for the crime. When Ellery questions if religion is a factor, Lincoln points to the nature of the crime—nailing someone to a cross. Mrs. Brad adds that while Tom was baptized in a Greek Orthodox church, he was considered an atheist who did not believe in rituals.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an investigation into the death of Mr. Braid, Detective Vane confirms the identity of the body by noting a strawberry-shaped birthmark on the right thigh. While Mrs. Braid insists her husband had no enemies and lived a secluded life, Jonathan Lincoln reveals a past conflict involving a man named Paul Roman. Lincoln describes Roman as a fraudulent figure who served as a disciple to a man named Harakat on Oyster Island, where they operated a nudist community. Lincoln notes that both Mr. Braid and a neighbor, Dr. Temple, disapproved of Roman's activities.

The investigation explores the local community. Dr. Temple, a retired military doctor, rents property from Mr. Braid to the east and is a frequent chess partner of the deceased. To the west, a British couple named Percy and Elizabeth Rein rents property that belongs to the Braid family. Mrs. Braid mentions that Mr. Braid was an exceptionally skilled chess player who once played to a draw against a national champion.

Ellery questions the witnesses about several names, including Varga Krossak, Clay, Andrew Farn, and Alroy, but receives no recognition. He also inquires about the timing of Harakat's arrival on Oyster Island, which Lincoln identifies as March, noting that Paul Roman arrived with him. Mrs. Braid reveals that Mr. Braid immigrated to America eighteen years ago with Stephen Megalos, suggesting they were former friends or business partners in Romania. Upon learning that the Braid family was once wealthy, Ellery concludes the questioning by requesting a map book from the district attorney, Mr. Escham.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After Lincoln retrieves a large blue atlas from the study, Ellery Queen examines its maps and indexes. Ellery reveals a pattern to the group, noting that several individuals involved in the case have used pseudonyms derived from geographical locations found in an atlas. He points out that Thomas Brad is a Romanian city, Stephen Megalos is a Greek city, and the deceased Andrew Van used a name corresponding to a city and lake in Armenia. Ellery observes that while Brad, Megalos, and Van used fake names to hide their origins, the name "Crocetta" does not correspond to any location in Europe or the Near East, suggesting it is a real name. Professor Yardley concludes that Crocetta is likely the real identity of one of the men behind the pseudonyms, which may provide the key to understanding the "hieroglyphic 'T'."

Following this discussion, the group moves toward the right wing of the house to visit the late Thomas Brad's study. They are met by the housekeeper, Mrs. Baxter, who serves them a lavish lunch. Professor Adler departs to return home for lunch, leaving Ellery, Escham, and Vawn. At two o'clock, the men enter Brad's study, a room featuring built-in bookshelves, a small piano in an alcove, and a desk with nearly full red and black ink bottles. Escham notes that they have already examined the desk with a magnifying glass without finding valuable files. He concludes that since the murder occurred in the gazebo, the only remaining element to study in the room is the chess set.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Vane informs Ellery that a red pawn was discovered near a totem pole. Ellery examines a round reading table in the room, where he compares tobacco ash from a pipe found in a gazebo to tobacco in a cigar box belonging to Brad. He discovers a hidden drawer containing a collection of high-quality pipes made of materials like sepiolite and celluloid. Vane presents a unique Poseidon trident pipe, which Ellery suggests was a gift because it is too unusual for regular use.

Ellery then examines a custom, wall-embedded foldable chess table with a mother-of-pearl edge. The board contains several black and red pieces, but Ellery notes that only eleven red pieces are present; the twelfth was the one found by the totem pole. Ellery questions why a wealthy enthusiast would use cheap wooden pieces for such an expensive table. The butler, Stollings, explains that Brad’s expensive ivory set was being copied by a carver in Brooklyn as a surprise for Mr. Temple, forcing Brad to use these temporary cheap pieces.

Stollings recounts that on the night of the incident, Brad instructed him, Fox, and Mrs. Baxter to go to bed early so he could be alone. Stollings observes that Brad appeared tense and was playing chess against himself. Brad initially sat in one chair but moved to the other chair after making a move with a red piece. Stollings and the others left in a car driven by Fox, who dropped them at the Roxy Theater. When Inspector Vance asks if Fox returned to pick them up, the narrative concludes.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an investigation at Bredwood Manor, the butler Stollings informs Inspector Vance and Ellery that he and Mrs. Baxter returned home via train and taxi around midnight after being driven to the Rockside Theatre by Fox. Stollings notes that Mr. Bred had previously stated he would be alone in the evening, marking the first time he has sent the staff and family away. Ellery examines a cigar box and confirms that the tobacco inside is a special English blend made by Mr. Bred, who refuses to smoke American tobacco. Stollings also identifies a Neptune-head pipe as belonging to Mr. Bred, noting the owner frequently rotates his pipes.

Officer Wain interrupts to report that a search of the scene on Oyster Island yielded nothing, specifically failing to locate the missing head of the victim, who is currently a headless corpse in the morgue. Vance explains that they have questioned everyone at the train station and local residents, but found no evidence of a visitor. While the district attorney suggests a criminal would avoid the nearest station, Vance notes there are no tire tracks on the gravel to suggest a car was used. Ellery suggests the visitor may have arrived via the channel by boat, a lead Vance begins to track.

After a further interrogation of an agitated Mrs. Baxter, which yields no new information regarding Mr. Bred's behavior or the presence of guests, the group moves to question Fox. They walk to a log hut in a grove of trees, where they encounter a tall, thin, muscular man who appears visibly shaken and fearful upon seeing them.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
District Attorney Escham and Officer Vance interrogate Fox, an employee at the location where a body was found. Fox, who possesses a naturally pale complexion, claims he spent the previous night driving around the city via Fifth Avenue, the park, and River Road, returning to Bradwood before midnight. He explains he did not pick up Stirling and Mrs. Baxter from the theater because no one asked him to. Despite Fox's calm demeanor and the presentation of a valid recommendation letter, the officers use a harsh interrogation strategy that leaves Ellery concerned by the tears in Fox's eyes.

Escham, Vance, and Ellery then walk through the woods near Kitcham Bay to investigate the property of the Lein couple. They find Percy Lein, a rugged British man, and his wife, Elizabeth, at their stone house in Cathedral Woods. Elizabeth maintains a stiff, frozen smile, which Ellery realizes is due to her wearing false teeth. The Leins claim they were in New York City the previous night, attending a show and staying at the Roosevelt Hotel because it was too late to return. They state they know almost no one in the area except for the Bradys, Dr. Addley, and Dr. Temple. During the conversation, the Leins express disdain for the people of Oyster Island.

The group then travels toward the water, where Ellery observes Oyster Island across the Long Island Sound. The island is described as a green, forest-covered landmass with a silhouette resembling an oyster and a single, decaying small dock.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Escham, Vance, Ellery Quine, and Officer Vaughan board a patrol boat to travel from the mainland to Oyster Island. As they cross the bay, they observe docks intended for the Reins and a replica dock on the eastern side of the island, where they believe Temple has landed.

Upon approaching the western dock of Oyster Island, the group witnesses a dark-faced man emerge from the shrubs carrying a struggling, seemingly unconscious woman. The man jumps into a motorboat and attempts to flee, but the boat's engine fails a hundred yards from shore. Simultaneously, a tall, muscular, naked man runs from the woods, curses at the departing boat, and plunges into the water to swim toward the escapee. When the police boat arrives with sirens wailing, the swimmer retreats toward the island and disappears into the woods.

The kidnapper, a wiry, sun-burned man named Victor Temple, laughs heartily as the police intercept him. The woman is identified as Hester Lincoln, who is covered only by a man's coat and sailcloth. After Ellery and Escham help Hester onto the police boat, District Attorney Vance informs the group of Breda's murder. Dr. Temple, reacting with suspicion and anger, reveals that he had gone to the island to bring Hester back to her senses because she had been living as a nudist. He expresses disdain for the nudist lifestyle, calling the practitioners charlatans, which causes Hester to react with bitterness. The police boat then heads back to Braithwaite's dock on the mainland.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Dr. Temple defends his decision to take Hester Lincoln away from Paul Roman, whom he describes as a treacherous scoundrel and a leader of the "sun cult." Temple claims he intervened because Hester is a confused teenager and that he had a disagreement with Roman earlier that morning. Temple expresses a desire to marry Hester, a sentiment she meets with a long, shining gaze.

The group, including Ellery, Isam, and Professor Yadley, travels by boat back to the island. During the trip, Dr. Temple reveals his background as a retired military doctor who served in Italy and was captured by the Austrians during World War I. Despite Temple's protests, Hester insists on returning to the island, defying his attempts to control her.

Upon landing, the investigators encounter Harakat, a disheveled old man in a white robe carrying a snake-headed staff, and Paul Roman. Harakat views the investigators as pagans. Officer Vane, acting as the district attorney and chief of police, confronts Roman regarding the murder of Thomas Bread. Roman claims innocence and states that he and Harakat have been on the island continuously, though Roman visited a village for supplies a week prior. The investigators follow Harakat toward a large, haphazardly constructed wooden hut. They observe that Roman appears to have given a warning, as Harakat's followers are already dressed and gathered in front of the structure.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Harkat, who calls himself Ra-Harakht, leads the group into a roofless sanctuary furnished with star charts, a statue of Horus, and various ritual objects. Professor Yadri reveals to Ellery that Harkat is actually a former world-renowned Egyptologist named Striker, who became mentally deranged following severe dehydration during an excavation in the Valley of the Kings. Yadri explains that Striker’s madness has caused him to muddle various Egyptian religious symbols and rituals together.

During the investigation, Detective Vane questions Striker regarding his departure from West Virginia Weldon. Striker claims he left five months ago following a "Cooper's Hill ceremony," which Yadri clarifies is a reference to an ancient Egyptian sunset ritual involving a specific type of cake. When Vane asks about a man named Croscott, Striker claims Croscott fled the sanctuary. Paul Roman, who met Striker in Pittsburgh in February, dismisses the religious aspects of the movement as a way to lure "rubes," admitting his true interest is promoting nudism for health benefits. Striker repeatedly claims that the arrival on Oyster Island was "all arranged," though he refuses to specify by whom.

The questioning reveals that twenty-three nudists, lured by advertisements and Roman’s proselytizing, live on the island. They are transported from a local train station to a dock on Dr. Temple's property, then ferried across by an old man named Ketchum. Inspector Vance gathers the terrified converts, preventing them from leaving the island while he verifies their identities. As the investigators prepare to depart, Dr. Temple urges Hester Lincoln to leave the group of "frauds, thieves, and idiots," but Hester remains standing beside Roman.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Following a physical altercation where Dr. Temple punched Rome in the chin, Hester flees into the woods in tears. Rome warns Temple to stay away from the island, threatening him with violence if they meet again.

The group leaves Oyster Island in a somber mood, contemplating the connection between the murder of a village school principal in Bredewood and a mysterious man claiming to be Harakht. Vane leads the police boat to the eastern end of the island to Ketcham's private harbor. While Dr. Temple and Professor Yadley remain on the boat, District Attorney Esham, Vane, and Ellery walk to a small log house to confront the landowner, an elderly man named Ketcham.

Ketcham reveals that a man named Cro-Sachs rented the island. This name provides a lead, as it matches a mysterious limping man from a previous murder case. Ketcham produces a typed letter dated October 30th from a "Wergha Crocetta," which included a $100 deposit for a summer rental starting March 1st. Ellery suspects the letter may have been sent by an associate named Harlock to mask Crosetti's involvement. Ketcham notes that while Crocetta never arrived, a man named Banesh and a man named Roman arrived on March 1st, paid the rent in cash, and stayed.

Ketcham further explains that Bredwood had been in conflict with the island's tenants, who are part of a nudist colony. Bredwood had attempted to bribe the tenants to leave or threatened legal action to evict them on the grounds of indecency, but Harakat and Roman rejected his proposals.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
An old man named Quetcham recounts past conflicts to Vane. He describes an argument three days prior where Mr. Bread crossed the bay to confront Striker in the woods. Roman, described as a man of great strength, intervened and forcibly removed Mr. Bread from the island, prompting Bread to vow to bankrupt his opponents to settle the score. Quetcham also details a fight involving Jonah Lincoln, who lives at Bread's place. Lincoln's sister stripped naked in front of Roman to protest her brother's control; when Lincoln slapped Roman in response, Roman threw Lincoln into the bay.

Following this, Vane, Professor Adley, and an agitated Dr. Temple return to the mainland via police boat. While Ellery and Professor Yardley spend a quiet evening discussing university memories, Dr. Temple departs his home in the middle of the night. He moves stealthily through the woods of Bradwood and toward the Lein residence. 

Crouching near a window at the Lein house, Dr. Temple eavesdrops on a tense conversation between Percy and Elizabeth Lein. They discuss something that has been buried and mention a "Bundy Reine incident" in post-war Budapest. Elizabeth expresses fear, while Percy insists they will be safe. After the couple falls silent, Dr. Temple retreats through the woods toward the pier. There, he observes a man and a woman in a rowboat near the shore. The woman pleads to be taken to the island for safety, but the man warns her that the situation is dangerous and that they should remain split up until the crisis passes.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At night, Paul Roman rows a boat away from a shore, leaving a trembling Mrs. Bradwood behind after a tense exchange where she promises he will not "get" another woman. Dr. Temple observes this departure from the bushes.

The following morning at the Braidwood estate, Ellery Quin finds the district attorney's car and police officers gathered at the house. Inside the living room, Detective Vance and Escham confront Fox, who is surrounded by Mrs. Braid, Helen, and Jonathan Lincoln. Vance accuses Fox of visiting Patsy Malone, a member of a New York gang, on the Tuesday night that Brad was murdered. Despite Fox's silence and apparent unease, the police subdue him and take his fingerprints. While Helen defends Fox's character, Escham notes that Fox may have been using false credentials. Vance reveals that the tip regarding Fox's movements came from an informant linked to Ellery's father.

The investigation shifts focus to the Braidwood family when Vance confronts Lincoln about a lie. Lincoln had previously claimed that Miss Braid and her mother returned together on Monday night, but a taxi driver's report reveals that only Lincoln and Miss Brad were in the vehicle. This revelation causes a confrontation between Helen and Lincoln, leading Lincoln to leave the room. Escham warns that everyone is now a suspect in Thomas's murder.

Later, Ellery visits Professor Yardley at his house, which features an ornate courtyard and pool. Ellery arrives with a message from Garcia to study with the professor.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Professor Adler review a stack of investigative reports regarding the deaths of Andrew Van and Thomas Braid. The autopsy of Andrew Van revealed no significant findings, and an investigation by the district attorney concluded there was no relationship between Van and the murdered millionaire Thomas Braid, despite similarities in their past correspondence. Dr. Rumsen’s report on Braid indicated no signs of violence, poisoning, or asphyxiation, suggesting Braid was killed by a gunshot or a blunt object to the head.

The investigation into the killer's escape route proved fruitless; police could not identify suspicious travelers by train, car, or boat in the Gulf of Ketchum on the Tuesday of the crime. Regarding the totem pole where Braid was found, the ropes used to bind him were identified as ordinary clotheslines, and experts noted the knots were clumsy but effective. Additionally, the background of a man who joined an Egyptology group in Pittsburgh is unknown.

Ellery discusses the suspicious Rein couple, Percy and Elizabeth, noting that Scotland Yard has been unable to find matching records despite their claims of British citizenship. Further findings reveal that a woman resembling Mrs. Brad left the Park Theatre alone on Tuesday night. A deposit check for renting Oyster Island was written by Vilga Croxall in Peoria, Illinois. While the Brad-Megal Company appears financially stable, Ellery notes that Stephen Megalos has been idle at sea for five years. Finally, Ellery discovers a suppressed report regarding Vilga Krossak, revealing that Krossak was an immigrant from Montenegro who arrived in America around the time of the Tutankhamun's tomb incident.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Erlie and Professor Adler discuss the background of Krulmit (also known as Crocetta), noting his history of small businesses in America. Four years prior in Chattanooga, Tennessee, Crocetta teamed up with a man named Harkness (or Stray) to manage a "sun cult" selling "sun pills" made of cod liver oil. Following the Alamo murder, Crocetta and Van's servant, Klint, disappeared without a trace. Erlie notes that while Crocetta investigated a potential alliance between himself and Klint, he found nothing.

The discussion shifts to Alva Strach, a famous Egyptologist who suffered heatstroke in the Valley of the Kings and is believed to be a harmless madman. Although the Hanover County District Attorney believes Strach is innocent of murdering Andrew Van, the evidence points toward Wilga Crosse as the killer. Erlie argues that the true key to the case is identifying the real identity of Wilga Crosetti, suggesting the person could be anyone, including Krosok, who had previously placed the Cult of the Sun near Bradwood. Erlie also theorizes that the motive might involve superstitions regarding vampires and decapitation, common in Central Europe and the Balkans.

Finally, the two debate the nature of a T-shaped symbol. Erlie believes the *crux commissa* is an "Egyptian cross" based on his research in the *Encyclopaedia Britannica*. Professor Adler corrects him, stating that while T-shaped symbols appear in Egyptian hieroglyphics, the term "Egyptian cross" is a misnomer. Adler explains that the true cross-shaped symbol associated with ancient Egypt is the ankh.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quine, Professor Yadley, and Adler discuss the potential connection between the crime scenes and the Egyptian Ankh, also known as the Crux Ansata. Elyot suggests that the killer, Croesus, deliberately transformed the Ankh—a symbol of life—into a symbol of death by decapitating the victims, thereby removing the "head" of the symbol. However, Professor Yadley refutes this, explaining that the Ankh likely originated from a representation of a shoelace rather than a statue. He notes that while the symbol eventually came to represent life, its physical form does not inherently imply decapitation.

Ellery subsequently rejects both the Egyptian and American totem pole hypotheses. He argues that if the killer intended to use the Ankh, the victims' heads would have remained intact, and he notes that the T-shaped symbol found near the crime scene lacks specific religious significance in Egypt. He also dismisses the Christian cross theory, noting that beheading was not a standard method for executing martyrs. Ellery concludes that the "T" shape may simply be the letter T, though he remains unsettled by the possibility of a coincidence.

The discussion is interrupted by an old woman informing Yadley that a drunk policeman is attempting to break in. Shortly after, Vance arrives with urgent news: a telegram from Jamaica. The telegram, signed by Stephen Megalas, states that he has heard of Thomas Brad's death and is traveling to New York immediately.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After experiencing engine trouble near New Providence Island, Stephen Megalas’s yacht, the *Helen*, finally reaches the Long Island coast on July 1st. The vessel is accompanied by a police boat and several small vessels filled with reporters as it enters Long Island Sound. This arrival follows the calm funeral of Thomas Brad, which was noted for the unusual display of grief from Brad's daughter compared to the fortitude shown by his widow. Meanwhile, a nationwide manhunt for Werger Croscak and a search for Clinton have yielded no results. A driver named Fox remains under strict, isolated surveillance in a small house; despite his silent and desperate demeanor, his fingerprints have not been found in eastern criminal collections.

At the Bradwood wharf, a crowd consisting of detectives, police, and investigators waits for Megalas. Megalas arrives via motorboat, accompanied by his captain, Swift, a weather-beaten man missing three fingers on his left hand. Megalas, a tall, tanned man with an Eton College accent, greets Helen tenderly but appears emotionally detached when discussing the murder of his partner, Tom. He is met by District Attorney Essem, Detective Vorn, Ellery Quine, and Professor Yadley.

During the procession toward the house, Ellery Quine asks Megalas if he has heard of Andrew Van, noting that Van was also murdered with his head severed and his body nailed into a T-shape. This revelation shatters Megalas's composure, causing him to turn pale and furious. Though Megalas implies he has an idea regarding the significance of the "T," he remains silent as the group enters the house.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Megalas identifies Wileg Croce as the individual responsible for leaving a "T" symbol, which he interprets as a symbol of life. After Detective Vance summarizes the murders of Van and Brad—including the discovery of the school principal's body and the testimony of the garage owner Crocker—Megalas insists on being taken to the gazebo.

At the gazebo, Megalas ignores the bloodstains and the T-shaped symbol to focus on a carved wooden pipe shaped like a sea god's head and trident. While investigators previously believed the pipe belonged to Brad (based on Stollings' testimony and Brad's fingerprints and tobacco), Megalas reveals the pipe is actually his own, which he had lost a year prior. Megalas notes that Tom (Brad) would never smoke another person's pipe due to an aversion to dirt.

Ellery Quin proposes a hypothesis to explain the discrepancy. He notes that Megalas has an impeccable alibi, as Captain Swift provides logbook entries and a Panama Canal receipt proving the yacht *Helen* was in the West Indies and the Pacific on June 22nd. Ellery argues that the murderer, Croce, used Megalas's pipe to stage the scene. By loading and smoking the pipe, the killer attempted to create the false impression that Brad was alive and walking toward the pavilion in a smoking suit before his death. Ellery concludes that Croce obtained the pipe from a desk drawer in the study.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Megala discuss the significance of a "god of the sea" pipe found in a drawer. Ellery initially theorizes that Crosetti left the pipe in the gazebo to falsely imply Brad was smoking there willingly, suggesting the gazebo was not the actual crime scene. He proposes that the real crime scene is Brad's study, where Brad was last seen alive.

Megala corrects Ellery, noting that Crosetti must have known the pipe belonged to Megala because it was kept in a uniquely shaped box engraved with Megala's full name in gold leaf. Ellery concludes that Crosetti deliberately left the pipe to create a temporary illusion that it belonged to Brad, but he stole the distinctive box to prevent immediate discovery. Ellery reasons that Crosetti's actions are designed to lead investigators to the real crime scene now, specifically because Megala's return will inevitably reveal the truth about the missing box. Following this logic, Ellery leads the group to the study.

In the study, which has been closed since the discovery of Breda's body, the group conducts a thorough search. While examining the room, Ellery moves a sofa away from the wall and discovers a dried black bloodstain on the floor. He notices a square indentation in the carpet fibers near the stain that does not match the rounded feet of the sofa. By moving the sofa further, he finds a matching indentation three feet away. Ellery concludes that the bloodstain was hidden because the carpet itself had been moved.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery discovers that the square-ended legs of a desk in the gazebo have left indentations on the carpet that match marks found under a sofa. He proposes an experiment to move the carpet to its position from Tuesday night to reveal a hidden bloodstain. Vane and Dr. Yadley realize that Crosetti (referred to variously as Croscot or Croasdale) likely intended to delay, rather than permanently hide, the discovery of the crime scene. Ellery notes that the bloodstain is located two feet behind a folding chess chair near the desk. He argues that Crosetti did not use a simple ink spill to mask the blood because he intentionally left a pipe as a clue to ensure investigators would return to the room.

Ellery dismisses two hypotheses: that Crosetti needed to remove something dangerous or add something to the room, because the police presence would have prevented such actions. Instead, Ellery proposes a third possibility: Crosetti wanted the police to find something currently in the study that was present on the night of the murder, but he intended for it to be discovered only when Mr. Meagher is present. 

To investigate, Ellery summons the butler, Stollings, to inspect the room. Stollings confirms that the room is identical to how it appeared before Mr. Brad's death, except for the missing chess table and the new bloodstain. Ellery concludes the discussion by instructing the group to split up and search for the object Crosetti intended for them to find.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
In a room belonging to the deceased Brad, Ellery, Professor Adler (Yadley), Wain, Esame, and Stephen Megalos conduct a frantic and seemingly fruitless search for a hidden object. While the men inspect furniture, lamps, and curtains, Ellery realizes that Crosack would hide something in a clever but accessible location rather than an overly complex one. Recalling a similar case involving the murder of Monty Field, Ellery's analysis inspires Professor Adler to examine the piano in the room. Adler reasons that because the room belonged to the dead Brad, no one would expect the piano to be played.

While playing the keys, Adler discovers a stuck note. Ellery uses a needle from his tool kit to probe the mechanism, retrieving a small, pressed piece of paper from between the ivory boards. The note is a message from Thomas Brad addressed to the police. In it, Brad expresses fear for his life and instructs the police to investigate the murder of Andrew Van, the headmaster of Arroyo College, who was found decapitated and nailed to a cross. Brad claims that Van is not actually dead and that only Stephen Megalos knows his location. He urges the police to keep this information confidential and to let Megalos lead the investigation to protect innocent lives from an "unscrupulous fanatic."

Upon reading the note, Megalos becomes visibly agitated and nervous. He checks a small gun, closes the door to the room, and expresses confusion regarding Van's survival and Brad's motives. Ellery observes that Brad likely intended the note to be found easily, but Crosack's thoroughness forced the discovery into a more hidden location.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery deduces that the killer, Crocecki, intentionally left a note at the crime scene after murdering Thomas Brad. The note stated that Andrew Van was still alive and that only Stephen Megala knew his location. Ellery and Eleanor conclude that Crocecki left the note to facilitate his own investigation; by delaying its discovery, Crocecki could search for Van privately before the police began a search. If Crocecki failed to find Van, the eventual discovery of the note would lead the police to Megala, who would then reveal Van's whereabouts.

Megala reveals that he, Brad, and Van were actually brothers whose real surname is Tvervel. They had assumed aliases years ago to hide their identity, a secret kept even from their own family members. Megala explains that the killer has been pursuing them for years as an act of revenge, using "T" symbols—such as a headless body and blood-written letters—to humiliate the Tvervel family. Megala notes that they have not seen Crocecki in twenty years and cannot recognize him, though he previously had a slight limp that may have been feigned. Ellery questions how Megala could know Van was still alive despite being out of touch with the world, to which Megala responds that the situation was part of a long-term arrangement related to the Arroyo incident.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery deduces that Andrew Tewel, Megala's brother, is alive and living under the alias "old Pete," a mountaineer persona used to evade Croscos. Ellery concludes that Croscos killed Clinton and believes they must act secretly to reach the Tewel brothers' location before Croscos discovers Andrew's true identity. Megala agrees to assist, motivated by his brother's safety.

That night, Stephen Megalus refuses to stay with the Brad family, choosing instead to sleep on the yacht *Helen* anchored off Oyster Island. Under police supervision, Megalus drinks to calm his nerves before sleeping. The following morning, Officer Vaughn arrives at the yacht and takes Megalus to the Breedwood dock. In a highly visible operation, Megalus and Vaughn are escorted by police and state troopers on motorcycles in a procession toward New York. To ensure the security of the move, detectives and police cars are positioned to block all land exits from Bradwood, while a boat monitors the water in Kagim Bay.

Meanwhile, Professor Yardley remains at his house with a special investigator, Eliot Quine, to maintain a facade of normalcy. Ellery had secretly departed Yardley's house on Friday night, picking up District Attorney Isam and driving toward New York. After a brief, exhausted stop at the Sennett Hotel in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, the two men continue their journey toward Pittsburgh in Ellery's Duesenberg, arriving in the Pittsburgh area by 3:00 p.m. on Saturday.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After a brief stop for food, Ellery, District Attorney Escham, and Isam arrive in Arroyo to meet Sheriff Rudden. They visit the sheriff's office located in the back of a building near the town hall. Ellery inquires about the whereabouts of "old Pete," a local man. Sheriff Rudden claims he has not seen Pete for two months and describes Pete's shack as being located in a desolate, difficult-to-reach area in the mountains near a cave region.

The Sheriff leads the group away from the town hall via a back street in the Duesenberg. They drive toward the mountains and park the car in a thicket, after which they begin a strenuous uphill climb through dense, sun-blocking jungle. Eventually, they reach a clearing where a crude shack sits under a large granite outcropping. The shack is surrounded by a thirty-foot-high protective fence made of tangled barbed wire, and it lacks a standard entrance.

To ensure secrecy, Isam bribes the Sheriff, who agrees to act as if he has seen nothing. As Escham and Ellery approach the fence, a man—identified by Ellery as old Pete—points a rifle through a barbed-wire-protected window and threatens to shoot them. Isam shouts that they are police and present a letter from Megalas to establish credibility. The rifle is lowered, and the disheveled, bearded old Pete opens the heavy, nail-studded door. Because there is no other way in, Pete instructs them to climb over the barbed wire fence to enter.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Isaac, Eleanor, and the district attorney Isam reach a neat, well-stocked mountain hut where they confront an old man known as Old Pete. After Esam presents a note regarding the murder of Tomasov, the man’s demeanor shifts from a hoarse, cataract-stricken mountain man to a well-educated individual. He removes a beard, a white wig, and face paint to reveal himself as Andrew Tewell (formerly Andrew Gaetzel), a tall, muscular man. Tewell explains that he, Stephen, and Tomislav were brothers. He expresses intense fear regarding a "terrible ghost" and suspects Krossak of the murders.

Tewell is initially suspicious of the visitors, refusing to speak until he sees his brother, Stephen. To alleviate his fears and facilitate travel, Isam provides a package containing a complete police uniform, shoes, and a loaded gun. Tewell dons the uniform, which fits well due to Megala's measurements, and the group departs the hut. They move through the twilight forest toward a clearing where DuSchenberg is waiting.

In Bradwood, the mysterious journey of Vawn and Stephen becomes a subject of community gossip. Later, as Ellery and Isam travel through South Pennsylvania toward Arroyo, a procession of motorcyclists and police cars arrives in Bradwood. Inspector Vance and a grim-faced Megala appear; Megala is escorted to his yacht, the *Helen*, where he departs accompanied by a police boat.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At a colonial-era house, a detective delivers a large envelope to Inspector Vance, whose expression turns stern upon reading the contents. Helen Bredwood interrupts, demanding an explanation regarding Stephen’s health. Vance dismisses her concerns, noting that Stephen mentioned groin pain, and Helen leaves to find Dr. Temple, who is currently sailing on a steamboat.

Vance and a detective proceed to a small cottage to interrogate Fox, the gardener and chauffeur, who is revealed to be Phil Pendleton. Vance confronts Pendleton with his criminal record, which includes theft at Vandervliet State Prison in Illinois, assault in California, and breaking and entering in Michigan. Pendleton maintains his innocence regarding the murder of Thomas Brad and expresses a desire to go straight. 

Pendleton confesses that an old prison associate tracked him down and threatened to reveal his criminal past to his employer, Mr. Brad, unless Pendleton met with a contact in New York. Pendleton claims he went to New York on Tuesday night, where a thug made him a criminal offer that he refused. While Pendleton refuses to disclose the specific details of the offer or confirm if he met Patsy Malone, Vance agrees to allow him to remain under police surveillance rather than in formal detainment, promising not to reveal his past to Mrs. Brad if he is innocent.

On Sunday morning at 9:15, while staying at Bradwood, Vance receives a confusing phone call from Stollings. After a brief, cryptic exchange with the caller, Vance hurries out of the house.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At 9:45, local prosecutor Isam arrives at Broadwood in a county vehicle with three police officers. While Officer Wainwright engages Isam in conversation as a diversion, Ellery drives his Duesenberg to the Addley estate. Ellery meets Professor Yardley, and the two walk to Broadwood. Wainwright informs Ellery that Megalos is on his yacht due to a groin injury, and that Dr. Temple has been on the vessel, the *Helen*.

A group consisting of Isam, Vane, Yardley, and Ellery boards a patrol boat to reach the yacht. Upon boarding the *Helen*, they encounter Captain Swift and eventually enter the main cabin, where they find Stephen Megalas suffering from a hernia testis. Dr. Temple provides temporary pain relief and departs via motorboat.

Once alone with the group, the "policeman" is revealed to be Andrew Jagg Tewell, a headmaster. Megalas and Jagg reveal they are members of the Tveril clan from the Black Mountains. They explain a generational blood feud between the Tverils and the Croscos family. Twenty years prior, Megalas, Jagg, and a man named Tomislav had set a trap to kill the Croscos father and uncles. The only survivors were a child named Vilga and his mother, who fled to Montenegro and Italy. Megalas explains that they have been tracking the now-grown Vilga across Europe to protect themselves, as they expect him to seek vengeance, but he had disappeared until his recent reappearance.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Andrew Van, a school principal, explains to Ellery and others the history of a Croatian blood feud that led his family to flee Montenegro for America. To escape a man named Croate (also referred to as Crowsack or Croscack), the three remaining family members—Andrew, Stephen, and the deceased Tom—sold their property, used fake names, and assumed different nationalities. While Tom and Stephen believed Croate was a myth, Andrew feared his return.

To protect himself, Andrew created a double personality by impersonating a mountain man named "Old Pete." He maintained an abandoned shack in the mountains and used disguise supplies to appear as the recluse in the town of Arroyo during his spare time. 

On Christmas morning, while living as Old Pete in his mountain shack, Andrew discovered a mutilated corpse nailed to a T-shaped cross at a local intersection. He realized that Croate had mistaken a man named Kline for Andrew and had killed him. To protect his own identity, Andrew decided to let Croate believe he had successfully killed Andrew. He swapped Kline's personal items with Andrew's own belongings to ensure the body would be identified as the principal. After burning Kline's items in a forest, Andrew waited for a farmer, Michael O'Keefe, to arrive so he could "discover" the body with a witness. Before heading to town, Andrew sent a letter to Tom warning him of Croate's revenge and stating that, for his own safety, Andrew Van was now dead and only Old Pete remained.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Megala informs the group that Tom wrote a note to the police as a final warning before Megala could return to Bradwood. Amidst the tension, Ellery Queen argues that the killer, Croscius (also referred to as Croscott, Crostach, or Crocker), is acting alone. Ellery bases this on the secrecy of the Tewell and Crosby family feud, Croscius's lack of funds to pay a co-conspirator, and the fact that Croscius left the letter "T" at crime scenes. Ellery also suggests that Crocker’s limp might be a pretense, as the Tewell brothers recall Crostach having a limp as a child due to hip disease, but it is unknown if it healed.

The police officer, Vorn, proposes providing heavy protection for the brothers, but Ellery warns that a large police presence would alert Croscius to the location of Andrew Tewell. Ellery advises that Andrew, currently disguised as "Old Pete," should be discreetly escorted back to his mountain cabin in Arroyo to avoid drawing attention. 

Andrew expresses a desire to renounce his inheritance from his brother Tomislav and flee to escape the "devil," but he eventually agrees to stay in character. Megala offers to advance Andrew five thousand dollars, noting that as the eldest brother, he is adhering to strict inheritance customs. Despite the offer, Andrew insists on using his own savings. Megala eventually sits at a desk to write a check for the money while Andrew G. Tewell prepares to depart.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After an exchange regarding cash and the Twer brothers' escape from Black Mountain, Officer Vane informs Andrew Ga Twer that he will stay overnight with the state police. As the group returns to the mainland, District Attorney Escham expresses skepticism regarding Stephen's explanation that the Twer brothers fled their homeland and changed their names simply because of a child's motive. Ellery suggests the brothers were actually fleeing a more direct danger and advises a transatlantic investigation, specifically suggesting a telegram be sent to Yugoslavia.

While walking around the east side of the house with Professor Yaredli and Escham, the group is approached by Dr. Temple and Jonah Lincoln. Jonah, agitated by the strange events at Bredehede, expresses frustration over his sister Hester's recent return from Oyster Island. A sudden, piercing cackle from the house leads Dr. Temple and the others to the second floor, where they find Hester in a state of hysterical madness. Dr. Temple and Officer Vane attempt to calm her, but Hester directs intense, hateful verbal attacks toward Mrs. Bradley before collapsing.

After Dr. Temple clears the room to administer a sedative, Jonah explains that Hester had been infatuated with a man named Roman on Oyster Island but has decided to leave for New York to be alone. When Elijah asks why Hester is so opposed to Mrs. Bradley, Jonah's demeanor shifts, and he firmly dismisses the question, claiming he has no idea.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Elijah and Ellery discuss speaking with Mrs. Bradwood before being interrupted by a detective. The detective informs Officer Vorn (referred to as Vance/Vorn) that Paul Roman and the Egyptianologist Strick are at the docks requesting a meeting. Jonah Lincoln, visibly angry, asks to accompany the officer. On their way, they pass Dr. Temple, who is preoccupied with a black bag.

At the docks, Roman and the shivering Strick demand to be released, claiming the police have locked their guests on Oyster Island and ruined their business. The "madman" Striker interrupts with religious outbursts, which Professor Yaredli dismisses as insanity. When Vorn refuses to let them leave, Roman becomes fierce but remains restrained. Vorn questions Roman regarding his whereabouts on the night Thomas was killed. Roman proposes that reliable witnesses could clear his suspicion.

As Roman begins to speak, Jonah Lincoln becomes extremely agitated, threatening to kill Roman. Roman eventually reveals his alibi: from 10:30 to 11:30 on the night of the murder, he was on a date with Mrs. Bradley, meeting her at Bridewood Pier to spend time by the seashore on Oyster Island. Vorn instructs Roman and Strick to return to the island while he investigates the claim.

Following the revelation, Jonah attacks Roman, citing insults to his sister and his influence over Mrs. Bradley. Roman retaliates with a powerful uppercut that knocks Jonah unconscious. Roman then jumps into Strick's motorboat and flees toward Oyster Island. Vorn pursues in a patrol boat. Later, the group gathers in a colonial-era house, where a recovering Jonah sits in a rocking chair while Ellery, Essem, and Professor Addley smoke in silence.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Officer Wain, sporting injuries from a recent brawl, arrives at the house and informs Lincoln that Sergeant Vane defeated a man described as a "giant." Following the confrontation, Jonah, Esam, and the constable head upstairs to conduct an official investigation. They enter the room of Mrs. Brad, who appears anxious and ill.

Esam and Wain question Mrs. Brad regarding her whereabouts between 10:30 and 11:00 p.m. on the night her husband was killed. They reveal that Paul Roman claimed she was with him near the docks, contradicting her claim of being at the theater. Under pressure, Mrs. Brad admits she slipped out of the theater during the first act, claiming to Helen she felt unwell. She took a train to Pennsylvania Station, got off early, and hired a taxi near Bradwood to return home secretly. She confirms she met Roman at the docks around 10:30 p.m. but maintains she saw or heard nothing unusual.

Helen Brad enters the room and expresses disdain for her mother's weakness in confessing. Helen reveals that she and Jonah noticed Mrs. Brad's suspicious behavior that night, and she had observed her mother lying in bed asleep before the body was discovered. When Ellery asks if she had suspected something for weeks, Helen confirms she had. After Mrs. Brad denies that her stepfather knew of the affair, District Attorney Esam ends the questioning. Later, while watching Independence Day fireworks on Long Island, Dr. Avery expresses disappointment to Ellery, noting that Ellery's detective skills have yet to produce a significant breakthrough.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Professor Yardley discuss the withdrawal of police presence from the investigation, which Yardley notes was ordered by the county sheriff. Ellery suggests that the police focus is limited to Stephen Megalos and Andrew Van (the Tewell brothers), noting that Megalos has protection through waterways and a squad, while Van utilizes disguises. Ellery mentions a meaningful connection to a chess game story that Yardley has failed to grasp, then leaves the professor to take a walk to clear his mind.

While walking through the dark countryside toward the Breadwood area, Ellery passes two detectives on a porch. He continues toward a path leading to a gazebo and a totem pole, but stops when he overhears a conversation near a tennis court. He discovers Jonathan Lincoln and Helen Brad engaged in a tense argument. Helen accuses Jonathan of cowardice regarding an encounter with a man named Roman. Jonathan, feeling unappreciated for his work for men who are either at sea or playing chess, declares his intention to leave his employment and go with Hester. He mentions having told Melchior his decision on a yacht earlier that day.

The argument shifts when Helen reveals that Stephen Megalos's year-long trip was actually due to his desire to marry her. Jonathan responds with sarcasm. Two days later, Ellery tells Professor Yardley that justice is merely taking a break, observing that Officer Vane is struggling with the lack of concrete evidence and acting belligerently. Yardley suggests Ellery should forget the case and relax.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quine feels uneasy as the investigation into the case seems to be winding down without a resolution, specifically wondering about Crosetto's motives. While various characters react to the ongoing tension—including Mrs. Bradley, Jonathan Lincoln, Helen Bradley, Hester Lincoln, and Dr. Temple—Andrew Van hides in the West Virginia mountains and Stephen Megala remains on his yacht. Scotland Yard sends a telegram stating that investigations into Percy and Elizabeth Rein in England have yielded no results, suggesting coordination with European law enforcement.

On a Thursday morning, Ellery and Officer Vane discuss the lack of progress. While Fox is mowing the lawn, he suddenly stops, runs toward the trees, and a scream is heard from the direction of the Rein residence. Ellery, Vane, and Fox rush to the scene and find Dr. Temple lying unconscious with a deep gash on his forehead. They carry him into the Rein house, which appears ransacked. Helen Bradley assists in tending to the injured doctor.

Officer Vane concludes that the Reins have fled the scene and contacts the authorities to report the assault and the Reins' disappearance. As Dr. Temple regains consciousness, he reveals that he had been attempting to crawl toward the house before blacking out. He then discloses a significant discovery: he believes the Reins are actually a couple known as the Tracetons. Temple recalls a theft of valuable gems in Budapest involving a German jeweler named Bondrein, and he identifies Percy Rein as the man he saw in Europe following his release from an Austrian prisoner-of-war camp.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Temple discuss the motives of the Reinke couple, suggesting they targeted Miss Bradley because of her status as a millionaire's daughter. Temple reveals that he had been searching for a hidden treasure for two weeks. This morning, while digging at a specific location, he was knocked unconscious by Reinke (also known as Tracdon), who stole the loot and fled with his wife. The group discovers a one-foot-square hole in the woods near the house.

Despite Inspector Vane’s efforts to arrest the couple, they remain at large. Vane sends telegrams to several European cities. On Saturday, three responses arrive: Paris identifies a couple matching the description as Percy Strong and his wife, wanted for robbery in 1925; Budapest identifies them as Traklund and his wife, wanted for jewelry theft since 1920; and Vienna identifies them as Percy and Beth Anlexter, wanted for defrauding a tourist and stealing jewelry. The authorities request the extradition of stolen goods. An officer notes that the fugitives' crimes have created an international dispute between France, Hungary, and Austria.

A telegram from Belgrade, Yugoslavia, explains a delay in reporting on the Twerly brothers and Vilga Krosak due to the dissolution of Montenegro and ongoing investigations into a blood feud. Meanwhile, Ellery and Professor Yadley discuss the lack of progress regarding the murder and the mysterious figure Crosetto. Later, Stephen Megalas arrives at a colonial-era mansion, confronting Inspector Vawn, Ellery, the professor, Helen Bred, Jonathan Lincoln, District Attorney Easam, and Captain Swift. Megalas demands accountability for the three weeks following Bred's death.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an emergency meeting, Detective Vane and the yacht owner, Megalas, engage in a heated argument regarding the progress of the investigation. Megalas criticizes Vane for failing to arrest the suspect, while Vane accuses Megalas and others of being suspicious and withholding information. Vane specifically challenges Megalas regarding a story about three men from several years ago, suggesting Megalas has omitted crucial facts. The tension is furthered by Wain, who accuses the group of wasting time, and Dr. Temple, who is criticized for his failed attempt to capture con artists.

To resolve the stalemate, Megalas proposes setting a trap for the anticipated attacker, Crozack. He suggests that the police should appear to withdraw their presence from the area to lure Crozack out of hiding. While Vane is skeptical that Crozack will not suspect a ruse, he agrees to consider the plan and promises a decision by the morning. Megalas expresses his determination to face the danger rather than hide on his yacht, the *Helen*. Ellery observes that Megalas is acting like a fool.

The following morning, the situation turns catastrophic. Captain Swift is found bound and unconscious in his cabin with a head injury. Megalas is found dead; his headless body has been tied to a mast on the deck of the *Helen*. The group travels to the yacht in a state of shock and nausea, witnessing the horrific scene of the murder.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On the yacht *Helen*, the body of Stephen Meagher is discovered tied to the ship's mast in a manner resembling a crucifixion. The corpse, dressed in bloody pajamas, is positioned against the radio operator's cabin, which features a mast structure shaped like a large letter "T." Professor Yardley and Ellery observe the gruesome scene, with Ellery noting the personal madness and terror inherent in such a ritualistic killing.

Officer Vane, focused on the incompetence of his subordinates, reprimands a lieutenant and a man named Bill regarding the failure to detect an intruder. A police boat discovers a dinghy floating in the bay, which bears markings indicating it belongs to a residential area near Bredewood. Wain suggests the dinghy belongs to Reine, prompting Vane to order a search of Reine's residence for footprints.

A knot expert named Rorshach examines the ropes used to bind Meagher and concludes that the knots were tied clumsily, like a beginner, rather than by a sailor. Vane notes that these knots are identical to those used on a clothesline and those used in a previous case involving a victim named Bradley. 

Vance informs the group that Captain Swift was also attacked; he was found unconscious in his cabin with a head wound. Additionally, Vance reports that a "T" was written in blood on the door of Meagher's cabin. The interior of the cabin is described as being covered in blood, as Meagher's head was severed on the floor. Dr. Temple arrives by motorboat to assist Dr. Rumsen and Rorshach in lowering the body.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Professor Yardeley suggests following bloodstains on a suspect's clothes as a lead, but Ellery argues that the killer, Croscot, likely planned the crime and prepared a change of clothes to avoid detection. Ellery predicts the suspect will be a limping man carrying a small suitcase or package. Detective Wain orders patrol boats to search for Croscot.

The group moves to Captain Swift's cabin, where they find him injured with a deep head wound. Dr. Temple treats the Captain's injury with bandages. Ellery notices a short-barreled semi-automatic pistol on a table near the Captain's bunk, though Vance notes it has not been fired. When questioned by District Attorney Eisman and others, Captain Swift explains that he and Megala rowed back to the *Helen* the previous night. Swift went to sleep at "seven bells" (11:30) after walking the deck. He reports that he woke up feeling something was wrong and heard heavy breathing beside his bunk. Before he could reach for his gun, a flash of light blinded him and he was struck on the head.

Dr. Rumson examines Megala's body and confirms his identity via a hernia, estimating the time of death between 1:00 and 1:30 AM. No items were stolen from the cabin or the safe. Ellery expresses frustration and professional despair, feeling he cannot grasp the significance of certain details, and suggests that the authorities prepare trawling equipment to search for Megala's head.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
A detective delivers a telegram from the Chief of Police in Belgrade, Yugoslavia, to Vane and the others aboard the ship. The telegram reveals an ancient feud between the Tverils and the Croscaks: Stephen, Andrew, and Timslof Tveril murdered Vega Croscak's father and two uncles before robbing the estate and fleeing to Montenegro. Ellery notes that this provides a motive for Vega Crocetta to murder the Tver brothers to avenge his destroyed family.

Another detective brings reports regarding the Rein family, though the sightings in Arizona and Florida remain unconfirmed. Meanwhile, a detective named Bill reports finding evidence near the dock: a rowboat with a rope cut by a sharp knife and five footprints in the mud. Bill observes that the footprints belong to a man with an eight-and-a-half-inch foot who walks with a heavy limp, as the right heel sinks much deeper into the mud than the left.

A middle-aged man named Elias Darling, a shopkeeper from Huntington, is brought aboard by police. Darling testifies that he saw a short man and a young woman park a Buick sedan in front of his store. Later, a tall, broad-shouldered man carrying a small travel bag opened the unlocked car, started the engine, and drove toward Central Harbor. Detective Pickard clarifies that a couple reported that specific Buick stolen in Huntington at two o'clock that morning. Ellery suggests that the suspect, Crosett, likely stole the car to facilitate his escape and then abandoned it.

title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On a police launch leaving Kaukau Bay, Ellery Quin, Vance, Isam, and Professor Yardley observe the body of Stephen Megalas being lowered from the radio room roof of the yacht *Sea Hawk*. Captain Swift and Dr. Temple are present at the scene. Ellery expresses a sense of failure, noting that the killer, Vilga Crocetta, remains an unidentified "ghost."

The narrative shifts to Bradwood in West Palm Beach, Florida. Ellery and Professor Yardley arrive at the residence, passing through a scene of grief and confusion involving Fox, Helen, and the hysterical wife of Bradwood. After being let in by Old Nanny, the two men retreat to the professor's study. Ellery struggles to grasp a fleeting, important idea that eludes him. To assist, Professor Yardley suggests that discussing unrelated matters or verbalizing thoughts can help an idea emerge.

The professor encourages Ellery to connect the testimony of Stolinsky with the arrangement of the chess pieces in Bradwood's study.
