Anthony suspects that the victim, Count Stanislaus, might actually be Prince Michael of Hesse-Darmstadt and suggests that the victim may have been an imposter. He asks Virginia to use her influence with the Earl of Carrington to view the body to confirm this theory. Virginia notes that the time of death, 11:45, aligns with a mysterious note, and she mentions hearing gunshots the previous night, though Inspector Bartlett claims no one else heard them.

To avoid being overheard by guests or staff, the pair rows out onto the lake. Anthony reveals his true identity, and Virginia admits her cousin, George Roxborough, had previously attempted to manipulate her into seducing a man named Jimmy McGregor to obtain a memoir. Anthony explains that he knew Virginia was not the author of certain blackmail letters because her psychological reaction showed she would not succumb to such pressure. The conversation concludes with Anthony revealing that one of the letters was mailed from the Chimney House, leading both to suspect that the letter-writer used Virginia's name intentionally.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia and Anthony discuss the potential murder of Michael, questioning if the Red Hand Gang is responsible. After rowing to shore, they reunite with the Earl of Carrington and Lady Bander. During their interaction, Lady Bander reveals that Miss White, a French governess, has been living in the second room from the left for two months. Anthony suspects treachery regarding this information.

As a taxi approaches the villa, Anthony recognizes a passenger as his old friend, Baron Lolopraitcher (Lolly). Following the departure of Bunter, Anthony hears a suspicious sound near the boathouse. He discovers a tall, bespectacled man with a pointed black beard attempting to hide. The stranger claims to be lost and seeks directions to the Happy Wanderer Inn, stating he is staying at the Happy Cricketer's Inn. Anthony finds the man's presence suspicious, noting the coincidence of another mysterious Frenchman appearing near the French governess's room, and wonders if he is involved in the Hessian uprising.

Returning to the villa, Anthony is introduced to the Baron and Captain Andreas by the Duke of Carringham. Anthony pulls the Baron aside to confess that he had previously used the false name James McGlashan. He reveals that he is the author of the memoirs the Baron was seeking. The Baron explains that the Prince orchestrated the retrieval of the manuscripts and intended to read and then burn them. The Baron suggests the memoirs have likely been burned, though he admits this is only a guess.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony discusses a missing manuscript with the Baron, suggesting the murder motive was to obtain it. Anthony asserts that he intends to deliver the manuscript to a publisher on Wednesday, October 13th, implying it was not burned as previously thought. While walking on a platform, Anthony encounters Herrmann Eschelbacher, who is lamenting the ruins of the grand villa. Anthony then slips away and discovers a hidden path between two cypress hedges. Inside the hedge, he finds Hiram Fisk, an American from New York, sleeping in a wicker chair.

Later, Anthony speaks with Inspector Battel (also referred to as Sheriff Bartlett or Inspector Bartholomew). Anthony expresses his frustration over being tricked and mentions his need to recover Stapleton's memoirs by next Wednesday. Battel suggests that a window in the crime scene might have been stuck rather than locked, a claim Anthony initially disputes before yielding. 

Hiram Fisk enters the room, identifying himself as a crime enthusiast and an author. He expresses interest in the room's art collection, including works by Holbein, Van Dyck, and Velázquez, noting he was invited by the Earl of Carrington to view first editions. Battel informs Fisk that no one can leave until an autopsy is conducted. As they move toward the living room, Fisk makes a comment about the Earl of Carrington. They encounter the Earl accompanied by Virginia Reeve. Anthony, recognizing her, prepares to observe her reaction.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia introduces Anthony Kidd to the Earl of Carringham, claiming he is an old friend she met in London. This social maneuvering allows Anthony to transition from a suspicious stranger to an accepted guest at the Chimney House. Anthony privately reflects on a gun he has hidden in a willow grove. During a walk by the lake, Virginia mentions that the murder at the estate is George's fault for inviting the current guests. Anthony suggests to the police chief that staying at the estate will make it easier to monitor him, a prospect that pleases the chief.

After a period of gongs signaling lunch, the Earl of Carringham invites guests into his study for drinks before moving to the dining room. During this time, Virginia whispers to Anthony that she convinced the Duke of Cambridge to show her the body, and she expresses her belief that Michael Prince is the killer. Anthony expresses interest in seeing the tutor, who is reportedly suffering from a headache, noting that he believes she is the person living in the second room.

The lunch is characterized by a somber and uncomfortable atmosphere. Following the meal, Bandler mentions that George will take a foreign delegation to a monastery to discuss national affairs. Mr. Fitch questions Anthony about the victim's identity, which Anthony evasively identifies as Count Stanislaus. Mr. Fitch criticizes the local constable's lack of initiative regarding the house lockdown and suggests the killer must have entered through an unlocked window. The passage concludes with Mr. Fitch departing to speak with the Earl.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While rowing on a lake, Anthony Kidd questions Bandel about her French tutor, Jeanne Viel. Bandel explains she hired Viel through an agency for a hundred pounds a year. Viel previously lived for ten years at the Chateau de Briodulle in Digne, France, in the house of the Comtesse de Briodulle. Anthony notes that Bandel has only corresponded with the Countess by letter and has never met her, which seems to satisfy his inquiry.

During their conversation, Bandel discusses the chimney house, noting that while it has historical significance, it is often used for boring tourist tours led by Mr. Tredwell. Anthony observes a melancholy man by the boathouse, whom Bandel identifies as Bill. After Bandel goes to meet Bill, Anthony plays "Indian" with two children, Giggles and Shimmer. Following the game, Anthony notices a black-bearded stranger hurrying across the courtyard. He then encounters Mr. Fitch, who appears flushed and out of breath, as if he had been running.

Chief Constable Bartle appears suddenly, and Anthony subsequently requests permission from Sheriff Balthazar to travel to the Chateau de Briodulle in Digne, France. Anthony plans to depart after tomorrow's autopsy and return by Sunday evening. The Sheriff grants permission on the condition that Anthony returns directly. Anthony expresses his suspicion that a specific memoir is the central cause of the ongoing troubles.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Sheriff Butler expresses a desire to cooperate with Anthony Kade, viewing him as a capable amateur detective. During their discussion, Anthony questions the succession of Prince Michael’s legacy. Detective Batterton reveals that Prince Michael's cousin, Prince Nikolai Ovaryevich, is the next in line for the throne and is currently in the United States raising funds. Anthony deduces that the death of Prince Michael creates a geopolitical conflict over oil rights between British-supported Royalists and American-supported interests, involving Azerbaijan's companies and George Rokhmats.

After leaving the discussion, Anthony visits a classroom where he meets Miss White, a middle-aged teacher. He feels foolish because she does not match his preconceived image of a famous adventurer. That night, a tall, muscular man named Boris Anschutz enters Anthony's room. Boris identifies himself as Prince Michael's former personal servant and declares his intention to serve Anthony for life, despite Anthony's refusal and claims that he cannot afford a servant.

The following morning, an autopsy is conducted on Prince Michael, streamlined by the police chief, Chief Constable Batterton, and a coroner. Following the examination, Anthony departs the chimney house. Later that night, Bill Everleigh is awakened from a dream by Virginia, who tells him she heard a loud bang and saw strange lights in the hall. Concerned by the bizarre occurrence, Virginia urges Bill to get up and investigate the situation.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bill and Virginia decide to investigate the presence of intruders in the chimney house. Bill arms himself with a fireplace poker and wears a red satin robe, though he struggles to move silently due to his iron-studded boots. Virginia questions why thieves would bother disassembling armor to steal it, noting that the house contains more valuable items.

The pair approaches the council chamber, where Bill peers through the keyhole and observes a flashlight illuminating the area near two armored statues and a Holbein portrait. Determining he cannot see enough to understand the situation, Bill plans to confront the intruders. He instructs Virginia to turn on the lights once he enters. As they creep into the room, they see a man's silhouette standing on a chair in front of the Holbein portrait, tapping on a wall panel.

Bill shouts for Virginia to "open" the lights, but the chandelier fails to work because the bulbs have been unscrewed. A violent struggle ensues in the dark, accompanied by the sound of an armored statue falling. Virginia screams for help, which brings lights to the foyer, but she sees a figure leap through a window and flee. She chases the intruder around the veranda but collides with Mr. Hiram Fisk.

Virginia recounts the burglary to Lord Carringham, Bender, and several servants in the drawing room. Trudwell restores the light by reattaching the unscrewed bulbs. While Virginia suspects there may have been a second person, Bill returns after a failed search, frustrated that the man escaped. Lord Carringham notes the room is in disarray, filled with broken crockery and armor shards, before preparing to retire.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After the Earl of Carringham retires for the night, Anthony arrives by train the following afternoon and meets Inspector Batters (also referred to as Chief Bettel/Balthasar/Bartle). Batters claims he is merely traveling to London, but Anthony questions his motives. Anthony discusses his failed hunches regarding the case, specifically his suspicion of a French au pair because her room light was on during the incident. Anthony also mentions a suspicious Frenchman named Chere, a silk salesman staying at the Happy Cricket Inn, whom he believes is part of a group involving the French governess at the villa.

Batters warns Anthony about the dangers of judging by appearances and mentions Victor King, a world-famous jewel thief and master of disguise, noting that Scotland Yard is searching for him. The conversation shifts to a news report about the unidentified, shot body of a foreign man found by a roadside, which Batters notes was clearly not a suicide. Anthony remains nonchalant, though he feels the Inspector is watching him closely.

While walking toward the mansion, Anthony re-evaluates the perspective of the lit window he saw on the night of the incident. He realizes his spatial orientation was uncertain and determines the room he saw lit might have been the third room from the end rather than the second. He asks the servant, Trudwell, who occupies that room and learns it is an American guest named Mr. Fitch. Trudwell also reveals that a failed robbery occurred the previous night, where thieves attempted to disassemble armor statues in the council chamber before fleeing. Anthony discusses this strange target with Mr. Isakov, who remarks that the attempt seemed amateurish and concludes with a threatening tone regarding the unfortunate situation at the estate.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Issacson and Anthony discuss the suspicious confinement of the group following the prince's death, which was officially attributed to heart failure. Anthony notes that Constable Batter's absence in London might be part of a plan, as the weapon used in the crime has not been found. Later, Virginia and Lord Caterham join the group, and Virginia invites Anthony to a secret meeting at the boathouse.

During the meeting at the center of the lake, Virginia, Bill, and Anthony discuss the previous night's events. Anthony suspects the intruders were searching for a small item rather than a memoir, noting that their interest in a wall panel suggested they were looking for a secret staircase. They discuss several suspicious individuals, including Esakistan, who has been sleeping excessively, and Perry, Michael's servant. Anthony predicts that the intruders will return tonight to take advantage of Constable Batter's absence.

The trio forms a task force to lie in wait. Anthony, carrying a handgun, watches the gate while Virginia hides behind a suit of armor and Bill stays by the window. In the early hours of the morning, a man crawls through a window and uses a flashlight to scan the room. As the man approaches the panel he had inspected previously, Bill is unable to suppress a sneeze. Anthony lunges at the intruder, and Virginia turns on the lights. The man is revealed to be a well-dressed, bearded stranger previously seen at the happy ballplayer inn. Sheriff Barter suddenly appears in the doorway, revealing he only pretended to go to London. The stranger, identifying himself as being from Scotland Yard, presents his credentials to Barter.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Inspector Bartholomew introduces Monsieur Le Mon, a spy from the Paris Security Bureau, to a group including Chief Balthar, Virginia Rue, Mr. Everly, and Mr. Kade. Virginia Rue, whose husband served in the diplomatic service during the assassination of King and Queen Hesslov, requests full disclosure of the situation. Chief Balthar agrees, noting that the matter involves secrets previously kept by Roamax.

As the group gathers around an oak table with food provided by Anthony and Bill, Chief Balthar explains that the current crisis stems from secret operations orchestrated seven years ago by the Earl of Stapleton. Le Mon reveals that a professional thief known as "King Victor" once operated in Paris under the alias Captain O'Neill. Le Mon connects this thief to Yvesse L'Immer, an actress from the White Hot Troupe. The Red Hand Party had originally hired L'Immer to seduce King Nicholas IV of Hesse to facilitate a political plot, but L'Immer instead seduced the King and became Queen Farajka of Hesse by posing as a relative of the Romanov nobility.

Following the assassination of the King and Queen by the Red Hand Party, it was discovered that many royal jewels had been replaced with counterfeits. Le Mon explains that Queen Farajka maintained contact with King Victor using coded English letters signed with the name of Virginia Rue to avoid suspicion. Chief Balthar states that while the Queen hid a specific jewel within Britain, its location remains unknown, though he suggests it may be in the room. Le Mon adds that during the chaos of the Hersovsky Revolution, Captain O'Neill was arrested in Paris, and the secret letters were stolen by emissaries from Hersovsky.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony, Sheriff Bartlett, Virginia, Lemont, and Bill discuss the connection between a mysterious package held by a man named Pedro and the Count of Stapleton’s memoir. Anthony reveals that McGrath, who provided him with the manuscript, claimed to know the location of the "Light of Heaven" diamond. McGrath also stated that the thugs who attacked him were from the court of Victor King, a detail that suggests a new motive for the murder of Michael King.

The French detective reveals that a man previously identified as Prince Nikolai of Hesse-Lobenstein is actually Prince Viktor, a fraud who recently fled the United States after a major scam involving oil rights. The detective believes Viktor has come to England and will eventually target the Chimney House.

Lemont explains his absence and his recent investigation at the villa. He describes witnessing a silhouette inside the room where Prince Michael was killed. The intruder was disassembling two armor figures and tapping on a panel beneath a portrait. When Bill arrived, the intruder turned off a flashlight and escaped through a window. Lemont had jumped through the window himself to avoid detection, which led to a chase where Bill and Virginia mistook him for the intruder. Lemont suggests the intruder might be impersonating a servant, possibly Boris Anskuov. Anthony dismisses the immediate danger of the intruder, noting that his new servant approached him of his own volition. The group concludes their discussion as dawn approaches.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After LeMone departs, Bates informs Anthony that the man found dead near Stanislas has been identified as Gisèppe Manelli, a waiter at the Hotel Britz. Anthony then confronts Balthazar (also referred to as Chief Batter or Sheriff Butler) regarding the Stanislas matter. Anthony admits that he was the one who moved the victim's body from the original crime scene to its current location.

Anthony recounts the events of the previous Thursday night to Balthazar, explaining his motive for coming forward: he no longer wishes to withhold information to protect Mrs. Rue, as it has been proven that the letters in question were not linked to her. Balthazar expresses skepticism regarding the jury's ability to believe such stories and admits he has been setting "small traps" for Anthony to catch him eventually.

The two men discuss the murder of Prince Michael. Balthazar believes the murder weapon is hidden within the villa or its courtyard and notes that searching the bird's nest might yield results. He theorizes that Gisèppe Manelli, a habitual thief, was hired by the Vicomte or the "Red Hand" to steal a memoir. When Manelli instead obtained letters and attempted to blackmail a lady, the Red Hand killed him for perceived betrayal. Balthazar suspects the Vicomte's involvement in the crime, specifically regarding the gun inscribed "Virginia," which he believes was an attempt to frame Mrs. Ruff. Anthony reveals he had identified "Virginia" as Michael. Finally, they conclude that the Victorians likely obtained the letters from Manelli on Pound Street, deciphered the code, and will return to the villa to find the diamond.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Bartle suggests that Anthony needs help from both himself and the deceptive Mrs. Reeve, Anthony returns to his upstairs room. There, he discovers an intact, unopened bundle of letters signed by Virginia Reveaux sitting on his vanity. Confused, Anthony questions how the letters returned to the villa and who placed them there.

At 10 a.m., the Earl of Carrington and his daughter, Bandel, have breakfast. Bandel questions her father about a man she saw eating alone. The Earl explains that he spoke with the sheriff, who stated that anyone wishing to leave the villa may do so, though the sheriff also hoped guests would stay. The Earl mentions he has invited "Kid" to stay and is arranging for Azerbaijan to take a 10:50 train to London. He expresses a desire to be rid of Fitch, whom he finds boring because Fitch never offers opinions. Bandel and the Earl discuss various guests, including George Romax and Virginia. The Earl expresses doubt regarding his relationship with Virginia, noting that while she is gentle, her words may lack true meaning.

Later, Mr. Hale Fiskle approaches the Earl to ask if the ban on leaving has been lifted. The Earl invites him to stay, and Fiskle accepts, citing an interest in studying the English countryside. The Earl then encounters Virginia in the living room. She declines his offer to accompany her to breakfast, stating she has already eaten in bed. The Earl invites her to stay longer, which she accepts. Virginia then goes into the garden, where she is startled by the sudden appearance of Constable Bartle. She remarks that despite a lack of sleep, the previous night was exciting.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Chief Batter (also referred to as Bates or Sheriff Bartle) meets Virginia Rue under a pine tree on the lawn to speak privately before he reports to Mr. Romax at Wivenhoe Abbey. Batter reveals that Mr. Kidd told him about the letters and the murdered man at Virginia's house. He questions Virginia about her history with Kidd, suggesting their first meeting was at her house on Pound Street. He also inquires about Kidd's past in Canada, Sudan, and his childhood, noting that Kidd only worked at a travel agency for a month. Virginia finds the Sheriff's sharp intellect terrifying.

After Batter departs, Bill Everly approaches Virginia and attempts to propose marriage, which she rejects, citing her age and status as a widow. Monsieur LeMon, a Frenchman, joins them for a walk. While telling stories about King Victor, LeMon appears to be secretly scouting the courtyard. He observes a luggage car approaching and hitches a ride to the village. A suitcase marked "H.I." falls from the car, and LeMon quickly returns to retrieve it.

LeMon opens the leather box and discovers various items, including bottles with gold lids and silk clothing. Hidden within a package of silk lingerie, he finds a heavy handgun. LeMon hides the gun in his pocket and instructs Bill to intercept the approaching Lancaster sedan to return the suitcase without mentioning him. Bill informs the driver, Eisakstein, that the suitcase fell out. Once the car passes, LeMon triumphantly reveals he has found the handgun. Meanwhile, Sheriff Bartlett reports to George Roxbury at Weinberg College.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
George examines coded letters that Anthony found on a dressing table. Chief Batter reports that Kidd’s background has been partially verified via a telegram to South Africa, confirming he worked for the Joice Travels agency in Bulawayo with Mr. McGrath. George expresses suspicion regarding Kidd’s demeanor and urges the immediate decoding of the letters. He suggests contacting a specialist, Professor Wood, who managed a department at the British Museum during the war; Batter notes that a telegram has already been sent to arrange his arrival on a train.

As George prepares to leave for London, he asks about Mr. Everly. Batter explains that Everly is sleeping after working through the night, but George requests that Everly be summoned to the monastery once he leaves. Batter shares a theory with George that King Victor the deceiver may have returned the letters to create trouble and lead investigators toward the hiding place of diamonds.

While driving Anthony back to the villa, Batter reveals that a gun has been discovered in Esakistan's suitcase. Although Anthony is shocked, Batter suggests this discovery aligns with his theories. Anthony is tasked with finding Everly, whom he observes walking with Mrs. Ruff. Later, Anthony speaks with Virginia, discussing his previous investigation into Miss White and the Countess de Bredouille de Dinard. The conversation is interrupted when Anthony notices Boris, a Hesslovac servant, watching them. Boris approaches Anthony and hands him a torn piece of paper containing a messy handwritten address, which he claims was dropped by a "foreign gentleman."

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony and Virginia walk through a rose garden toward a villa, discussing a mysterious man who behaves like a hunting dog and recently provided a paper allegedly dropped by a foreigner, whom Anthony identifies as Lemmon. Anthony expresses that he does not regret his involvement in the current trouble, despite Virginia’s concerns. During their walk, Anthony shares his cynical views on risk-taking, marriage, and the necessity of compromise to achieve one's goals. He confesses his desire to make Virginia like him, noting that he felt a "magic" from her when they first met on Pound Street.

Virginia points out that Constable Butler is watching them from the library window. Anthony waves to Butler, who informs them that Professor Woodward has arrived to decode a cipher on a letter. The group observes the red-haired Professor Woodward through the window; he dismisses the decryption task as simple common sense and announces he has finished decoding the letter marked "Chimney House." After the professor departs, Butler provides Anthony and Virginia with a summary of the Queen's letter. 

The decoded text reveals that a person identified as "S"—whom Anthony identifies as Stapleton—has moved a diamond from its hiding place. The letter contains a note: "Richmond, seven straight eight left three right." While Anthony and Virginia initially consider if the diamond is in Richmond, Virginia realizes the diamond is likely hidden in the villa behind a Holbein portrait in the dining room, specifically near the portrait of the Earl of Richmond. Butler notes that thieves had previously searched the armor figures beneath the painting, suggesting the numbers in the note might refer to measurements for a secret panel or passage.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia, Bunder, Anthony, and Chief Constable Bates discuss the existence of secret passages in the house. Bunder explains that a passage once connected the chimney villa to Weimar Abbey but is now mostly blocked, though she notes that a passage under the White Gallery and a priest's room are also significant. She offers to show them a hinged panel leading to the council chamber after lunch.

At 2:30, Bunder, Virginia, Chief Bardell, Mr. Lemon, and Anthony gather in the council chamber. Bunder suggests they are searching for the Prince's historical diamond, which she claims was stolen when she was a child. Bunder reveals a hidden spring beside a Holbein portrait that opens a panel to a dark passage. Bartholomew and Lemont lead the group into the tunnel with flashlights. The passage is brick-lined and approximately a hundred yards long before it becomes blocked by debris.

Bartholomew provides a series of directional steps to navigate the area. Following a sequence involving seven steps, eight bricks from the left, and three bricks to the right, a detective uses a knife to extract a unique brick. Behind the brick, they discover a small hole containing a row of pearl buttons, a piece of coarse knit fabric, and a note with the letter "E" written on it. Anthony suggests these items are a riddle or code left by the late Count Stapleton to mislead thieves. Lemont discovers a fresh match on the ground, suggesting the passage has been used recently, despite Bunder’s claim that it had been untouched for two years. After the group exits through the panel, the Marquis of Carrington expresses annoyance at the commotion. Anthony pulls Lemont aside to discuss a piece of paper given to him by Packard.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony examines a scrap of paper with an address, which Leemann claims he has never seen before. Leemann suggests the paper may have fallen from Essex's suitcase and advises Anthony to investigate Pourezzi, a servant of Michael Prince, noting that Victor could be impersonating a servant. Leemann expresses a preoccupation with capturing Victor King, stating his purpose in England is to find him rather than solve the diamond theft or Prince's murder. Anthony notes that Leemann has already recorded the address on his shirt cuff.

Later, Anthony speaks with Chief Constable Bartlett (also referred to as Sheriff Barton), who is troubled by the lack of clarity in the ongoing investigation. Bartlett reveals he has received an anonymous, misspelled note warning that Anthony is "not what he sees." Anthony dismisses the note and retreats to the villa, where he becomes solemn and decides he must act. He heads to the rose garden, where he encounters Mr. Fuchs. After a polite conversation regarding rose varieties, Miss Bandler invites Mr. Fuchs to drive to London.

As Bandler's car departs, Anthony suddenly changes his demeanor and races through the courtyard to intercept the vehicle. He vaults over a wall and jumps into the passenger seat of Bandler's Panhard, announcing he is going to London. Bandler notices Anthony is holding a match, which he pockets after examining it. As they drive at high speeds through the countryside, Bandler questions Anthony's sudden departure and whether he is escaping the law, musing that the Scotland Yard man is not as weak as expected.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While driving to Oxford Street, Bandel and Anthony discuss Virginia. Bandel claims to have inside information regarding Virginia’s marriage to the late Tim Ruff. She asserts that Virginia never loved Tim, despite his intense passion and threats of suicide, and that Virginia only married him because of his overwhelming devotion. Bandel notes that Virginia is highly intelligent, helped Tim's career in Hersovlav, and deeply values her freedom. After Bandel drops Anthony off at the Hyde Park corner, Anthony takes a taxi to Victoria Station and eventually travels to Dover.

Upon arriving in Dover, Anthony seeks directions to Hirstmill, the last house on Langleys Lane. He finds the house abandoned and secluded, surrounded by overgrown gardens. While approaching the property stealthily, Anthony observes a short, stocky foreign man acting as a guard. He follows the guard to an open area and peers through a window, discovering six men gathered around a table. The group consists of four robust men with Magyar features and two small, agile men. The men speak French and engage in a heated argument regarding their "boss," whom they are waiting for. One man identifies the leader as Victor, noting that Victor does not tolerate defiance. The men also mention a prisoner whose head wound has healed. Anthony, observing the group's lack of discipline and the tension between the different factions, decides he wants to give "King Victor's" headquarters a lesson.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After hearing a muffled sigh, Anthony climbs through a locked window into a dim room. He discovers a pale, emaciated foreigner with a bandaged head, whose hands and feet are tied. As Anthony approaches, Mr. Hiram Fisk appears at the door, holding a large blue automatic pistol and commanding Anthony to raise his hands, noting they were on the same train at Victoria Station.

On Tuesday evening, the Earl of Carringham, Virginia, and Bondel discuss Anthony’s sudden departure in the study. Lord Carringham expresses his unease regarding the lack of security and the disappearance of Fischer, who was last seen in the rose garden. Tredwell interrupts to announce that the French detective, Monsieur Lemont, has arrived.

Lemont informs the group that he has made a significant discovery regarding a suspicious guest who arrived from a remote location via South Africa. Virginia challenges Lemont, arguing that Victor von Winkle (Mr. Fuchs) is more suspicious than the missing Mr. Kede, citing his secretive behavior and presence near the scene of the murder. Lemont counters by revealing that Mr. Fuchs is actually a private detective for Pinkerton who came to the house to follow Victor Wang. 

The discussion turns to the pursuit of a target, with Lemont expressing determination to catch him. Virginia defends Anthony Kede, swearing he did not kill Prince Michael. Lemont suggests an alternative possibility: that Boris the Hessian killed the Prince out of revenge for past wrongs.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Lemont informs the Earl of Carrington of the case's progress, Bander expresses disdain for Lemont's appearance. As Virginia Rue heads to bed, she spots Chief Inspector Batter leaving through a side door. She questions him about Lemont's claim that Mr. Fisk is an American detective, which Batter confirms. Disturbed by this realization, Virginia retreats to her room to contemplate Anthony's previous words.

At 1:30 a.m., a figure taps on Virginia's window. She discovers it is Packard, who is acting on behalf of "the master." Packard tosses her a note from Anthony stating that he is in danger and attempting to escape. To avoid the detectives stationed near the conference room, Packard instructs Virginia to exit through the side door, where he will be waiting with a car. Virginia changes into a yellow sweater and cap, leaves a note for Bunder on a needle cushion, and slips out of the villa.

On Tuesday, October 13, at 10 a.m., Anthony Ked visits Baron Lothrop Loyd at the Hotel Harijgi. Accompanied by a suspicious Captain Andrassy, the Baron listens as Anthony proposes a business deal based on supply and demand. Anthony cryptically offers to provide a "prince" to serve as the backbone for the Baron's political plans. Anthony invites the Baron and Captain Andrassy to the Chimney House at 9 p.m. to discuss the matter further. Anthony then travels to London to seek an urgent meeting with Mr. Issakov. After mentioning the Chimney House to a military aide, Anthony is granted an immediate audience with Mr. Issakov, who offers him a cigar.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony meets with Mr. Azerbaijan (also referred to as Isakov or Isaacstan) to discuss a deal regarding Hesslegrave and oil rights in Eisakstan. Anthony proposes finding a pro-English heir to the throne to secure these rights. They agree to a cash deal under the same loan terms previously offered to the late Prince Michael. When Isaacstan refuses Anthony's request to meet at the Chimney Villa that night due to a dinner engagement, Anthony reveals that the gun used to kill Prince Michael was found in Isaacstan's suitcase. Although Isaacstan claims this is a lie, Anthony whispers a suggestion in his ear that leaves him stunned.

Later, at nine o'clock, a gathering takes place in the council chamber of the Chimney Villa. The Earl of Carrington expresses weariness regarding the group, which includes Bunder, the Earl of Chatham, George Romax, Everleigh, Bill, and Bandel. They discuss the disappearances of Mr. Fischer and Virginia, noting that Virginia left a note. The group observes a mix of detectives and officials, noting the absence of Victor King (Kidd), whom they suspect is orchestrating the meeting from afar. Anthony arrives and announces that secrets will be revealed. He explains that a previously undecipherable cipher can be solved using the memoirs of the late Count Stapleton. By connecting the Count's rose emblem to a "flower" theme and identifying rows of items, Anthony concludes that the cipher refers to the Earl of Carrington's library. He asserts that the hidden jewels are concealed behind or within a fake book titled "The Earl of Richmond."

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following Anthony Kidd's clever deductions, a tense confrontation ensues between Anthony and the French detective, Lemont. Lemont accuses Anthony of being the criminal Victor King, noting that Anthony was in South Africa two months prior. Lemont reveals he has requested fingerprint cards and a body size chart from France to compare against Anthony's physical features to prove his identity. Anthony dismisses the accusations, arguing that even if he were Victor King, his past crimes are legally settled or difficult to prove, and he suggests he is merely trying to live a quiet life. Chief Constable Bettlebutt and Lemont further press Anthony, accusing him of fraud regarding Prince Nicolas and involvement in the murder of Prince Michael.

Anthony claims to have a "trump card" in the form of a person he has been keeping upstairs. As a whistle signals from the corridor, Anthony leads the group—including the Earl of Carringham and Lemont—to the library window. Inside, they observe a shadowed figure searching the bookshelves with a flashlight. A violent struggle breaks out, and after the lights are turned on, the group discovers Percy wrestling with a woman. A gunshot rings out, and the woman falls dead. Percy, visibly enraged, declares that the woman killed his master on the orders of the Duke of Michael and that the gun went off accidentally during their struggle.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
George Romax and others discover the body of a woman holding a gun; Anthony identifies her as Miss White, though she was actually an actress impersonating the real Miss White, who had been kidnapped. Anthony explains that the woman was an imposter who had been hiding for years following a revolution and a reported murder at a palace. She had been searching for a diamond and shot Michael Prince after he accidentally recognized her. To deflect suspicion, she had placed the gun in Eisakstan's suitcase and returned letters to mislead investigators.

The Baron reacts with shock to the woman's identity, claiming she was supposed to be dead, but later whispers a secret to George Romax that leaves George visibly shaken. Amidst the confusion, a man claiming to be LeMon is revealed by Anthony to be a French secret agent. Simultaneously, Hiram Fisk intercepts the real LeMon, stating he has been waiting to arrest him.

In a later explanation, Anthony reveals his strategy: he used the suspicion surrounding Prince Victor and the identity of the "King of Viktor" to manipulate the situation. He intentionally fed information about jewels and "the row of things" to LeMon to confuse him and his accomplices. Anthony also explains that while LeMon was busy accusing him of being the King to keep everyone distracted in the room, a note was delivered to Miss White, and the gem was stolen. George Romax criticizes Anthony's reckless methods, noting that his plan nearly resulted in the loss of a national treasure.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Fuchs informs Mr. Römer that the historical artifact was not hidden in the library books, as previously believed. Anthony explains that the artifact's location is linked to a rose, specifically a red rose bush named Richmond in the garden. By facing the sundial and walking seven steps forward, eight steps left, and three steps right, one reaches the bush. Anthony reveals that the library story was a fabrication used to lure Miss White into a trap, while he and Mr. Fitch had established a military cordon in Dover to prevent the Red Hand from contacting an imposter named Lemmon.

The financier, Mr. Esakistan, questions Anthony's presence, stating he came to discuss a loan with Prince Michael of Hesse. Anthony reveals that he is actually Prince Nikolai Szejewsky Ferdinand Opolovitch, having previously used the alias Anthony Kade in Congo after spreading news of Prince Michael's death. The Baron accepts Anthony's identity, noting his resemblance to his British mother. Anthony explains that his position is dangerous because he is the heir to the throne, which provides motives for others to eliminate Michael O'Paul.

Anthony further discloses that he married Virginia Rufe, a British noblewoman, that very morning. After the others depart, Anthony speaks privately with Chief Batterton. He confirms that the deceased lady was Queen Faraja and explains his past decision to hide his identity due to his youthful democratic ideals and his preference for his British upbringing. Anthony admits he is accepting the role of king primarily for his wife, having arranged terms that satisfy both the Baron and Mr. Esakistan regarding the need for a monarch and oil.

title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony discusses his loyal servant, Berceuse, with Bartel, the chief of police. Anthony notes that Berceuse’s instinctual loyalty to the House of Paulovitch is remarkable. After Berceuse leads Anthony outside, Anthony encounters Virginia and Jimmy McGregor. Jimmy explains he traveled to join Anthony after being attacked with a knife following a failed attempt by Europeans to buy a manuscript.

Anthony reveals he has the real memoir manuscript, which he had hidden in a hotel safe after creating a fake one to deceive a publisher. He tasks Jimmy with delivering the package to Mr. Benson’s secret address in London for a reward of one thousand pounds. Anthony notes the manuscript is politically dull and lacks interesting anecdotes. Anthony then confesses his true identity to Virginia: he is the real Prince Nikolai of Hesse's Land. He tells her they will travel to Hesse's Land to become king and queen, jokingly noting the low life expectancy of monarchs there. After Jimmy departs to complete the delivery, Anthony and Virginia share a romantic moment.

The narrative shifts to Thursday morning at Chimney House, where Officer Johnson digs a symbolic "grave" during a somber gathering involving George Romax, Chief Batter, and Mr. Fitch. Following a formal procedure, George Romax examines the contents of a small bag. Elsewhere, the Duke of Cambridge urges his daughter, Benedict, to drive him to London immediately, expressing his desire to sell the chimney house to a consortium if the country does not buy it. Meanwhile, Bill Everly makes a phone call to Mott, and Mr. Fisk reflects on the eventful week.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At the Chimney House, a young man named Jimmy Sycamore runs down the stairs and collides with the butler, Mr. Truddwell. Jimmy arrives late for breakfast at 11:15 AM, drawing a reproachful look from the landlady, Mrs. Cutt. Mrs. Cutt, a tall and beautiful woman with a sorrowful demeanor, views tardiness as a significant sin due to her past marriage to Sir Osbert Cutt, who demanded strict regular habits. Although she has achieved high social status through Sir Osbert's success, she feels isolated in the large, rented mansion known as Smokestack Villa.

Seeking interaction, Mrs. Cutt approaches the head gardener, McDonald, on the terrace. She first asks to pick ripe grapes for dessert, but McDonald firmly informs her they are not ready. She then proposes converting the lawn behind the rose garden into a croquet lawn for Sir Osbert's enjoyment. McDonald refuses, using the prospect of involving "William" as a deterrent to coerce her into dropping the suggestion. Mrs. Cutt quickly surrenders to his authority.

After finishing a meal of kidney and bacon, Jimmy joins Mrs. Cutt on the terrace. They discuss the whereabouts of the other residents, specifically noting that Mr. Wade has not yet come down for breakfast. Mr. Truddwell notes that Mr. Wade did not arrive until 11:30 PM the previous day. As Mrs. Cutt expresses concern over the lateness, a serious young man wearing glasses enters the room.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Rupert Bettman, the private secretary to Sir Osborne, is summoned by Mrs. Cutt. While Bettman—nicknamed "the chimpanzee" by his former schoolmate Jimmy Cessie due to his serious nature and glasses—departs, Jimmy joins a group by the lake. The group includes Bill Everly, Ronnie DeVille, and three girls: Helen, Nancy, and "Socks." They discuss their plan to play a joke on Gerry Wade, who has overslept. Ronnie suggests a loud commotion to wake the entire house, while Socks insists on being "subtle."

Bettman arrives and suggests using an alarm clock to wake Gerry. Following this, the group decides to buy multiple alarm clocks. Jimmy investigates the restaurant and finds Gerry eating toast and jam. To prevent Gerry from joining the group, Jimmy, Nancy, and Helen enlist the help of a bewildered Mrs. Cutt. While they attempt to distract her, Gerry emerges and engages Mrs. Cutt in a conversation about punctuality and gardening to avoid her lectures.

Meanwhile, Bill, Ronnie, and the girls travel to the Basing Mall. They encounter the store owner, Mr. DeGroatroyd, who attempts to sell them high-quality timepieces. Ignoring his advice for reliability, the group specifically requests loud clocks. Jimmy adjusts the settings on several models, causing the store to echo with deafening alarms. The group eventually selects the six loudest alarm clocks, with Ronnie noting they will buy one for Bettman as well.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After purchasing several alarm clocks, Bill, Jimmy, and others plan a prank against Gerry Wade. The group gathers in Gerry’s bedroom to hide the clocks. A debate ensues regarding whether the alarms should all ring simultaneously or sequentially; they decide to set them to go off one after the other, starting at 6:30 am. When Mr. Bettman suggests that Gerry might hear the ticking of the clocks once he is in bed, the group—referring to a member as a "chimpanzee" for his intelligence—decides to wait until Gerry is asleep and then sneak in to place the clocks on the floor.

Meanwhile, a bridge game continues. Osborne-Smith and Rupert Bettman play with high concentration, while Gerry Wade and Mrs. Cutt play a more casual game. Sir Oswald and Lady Cutt are paired together; Sir Oswald frequently criticizes Lady Cutt's play, though she remains gracious. During the game, Lady Cutt wins a trick and collects winnings from Sir Oswald, including ten shillings.

At 12:30 am, the guests begin to depart. Ronnie DeVille, who lives next door to Gerry, acts as a lookout. At 1:45 am, Ronnie informs the group that Gerry’s light has gone out and he appears to be sleeping soundly. The conspirators gather in the hallway to finalize the plan. To avoid being too noisy, they decide to pass the clocks into the room one by one rather than entering as a group. After eliminating several candidates due to their size or tendency to giggle, the group selects Rupert Bettman as the most suitable person to enter the room.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
A group of conspirators, including Jimmy Sexton, Socks, and the Chimpanzee, execute a prank by placing eight of Mr. Dumplin's alarm clocks near Gerry Wade's bed. The Chimpanzee carefully places the alarms to ensure they ring loudly without waking Gerry. When the alarms go off at dawn, Ronnie DeVille is startled, but Gerry remains asleep. The conspirators speculate that Gerry is either playing a trick on them or has a medical issue, such as damaged eardrums, because he fails to react to the deafening noise.

By noon, the group is disappointed that Gerry has not appeared. Mr. Truddwell, the butler, enters to inform them that Williams went to check on Gerry and found that he had "slept through." Truddwell reveals he has already locked Gerry's door, summoned Dr. Carruthers, and intends to notify Sir Osward. Ronnie is visibly shaken, and Jimmy attempts to console him on a terrace, privately wondering if the prank has turned into a tragedy or a murder.

Dr. Carruthers informs Ronnie and Jimmy that Gerry died from an overdose of chloral hydrate, noting a bottle and cup near the bed. While the doctor suggests the death might be a suicide, Ronnie firmly denies that Gerry would do such a thing. The doctor inquires about potential motives involving money or women, but Ronnie denies any such troubles. Ronnie learns that Gerry has a half-sister living at Dean Abbey and volunteers to notify her. He asks Jimmy to drive him there in his car.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy and Ronnie DeFleur drive to Dean's Priory after Ronnie hesitates to share information regarding Gary Wade. During the drive, Ronnie asks about Gerry’s sister, and Jimmy describes her as remarkable. Upon arriving at the Priory, they meet Miss Wade, a petite girl with blue eyes and two black, long-eared dogs. Jimmy introduces Ronnie, and Ronnie informs Miss Wade that Grimm is dead. Although she initially questions if Gerry died from sleeping pills, she eventually asks for details regarding the death. Miss Wade expresses a desire to be alone and states she will arrive at Chimney House later in her two-seater sports car.

Returning to Chimney House, Jimmy encounters a tearful Mrs. Cutt. Ronnie, having just left Greta Wade's room, insists that Jimmy enter the room to pay his respects to the deceased. Inside, Jimmy observes Gerry Wade's pale, motionless body on a bed covered in white flowers. He notices a row of clocks on the mantelpiece and discovers that there are only seven clocks instead of eight, prompting him to wonder why one is missing.

In a separate scene, Lord Kettlemere discusses the nature of successful, self-centered people with his daughter, Miss Elinor Brent (known as "Bondel"). They discuss land reclaimed from Old Quart. Lord Kettlemere expresses his distaste for oppressive, strong-willed individuals and mentions his opposition to autopsies, noting that a similar trouble occurred four years ago due to George Lomax.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lord Kettlem and Bondel discuss the death of Gerry Wade, which occurred in Bondel's room. While the police, specifically Detective Largen, suspect a political motive or non-normal cause, the death is officially considered an accident or suicide. Tredwell reported that investigators found only Wade's fingerprints. Lord Kettlem mentions that Wade has a much younger half-sister.

Bondel attempts to assert authority over the gardener, McDonald, by demanding he clear the overgrown tennis court grass and pick grapes out of season, though he responds ambiguously. Later, Lord Kettlem reveals details regarding an "alarm clock incident": vacationers had reportedly hidden numerous alarm clocks in Wade's room as a joke. Following Wade's death, the clocks were found arranged on a mantelpiece, though the servants denied touching them.

That night, Bondel reflects on the strangeness of the clocks. She recalls a detail from her maid: seven clocks were arranged on the mantelpiece, while an eighth clock was found in the grass outside the window. Contemplating whether Wade threw the clock before dying, she concludes it was unlikely given his state of unconsciousness. Seeking clarity, she decides to contact Bill Everleigh.

While attempting to write to Bill, Bondel finds a wrinkled, unfinished letter stuck in her desk drawer. The letter is dated September 21st, the night before Wade was found dead. Addressed to Lorena, the note mentions that Wade is feeling well but tells her to forget about "the seven clock thing," which he previously thought was a joke. The letter ends abruptly after Wade begins to mention someone named Rachel. Bondel notes two specific phrases in the text: "I'm feeling well" and "I'm so tired, my eyes won't stay open."

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondel reflects on the death of Gerry, who died after taking a large dose of chloral hydrate, questioning why he would take sleeping pills if a specific letter was true. While sitting in a room where Gerry died, she is unsettled by the ticking of her golden clock and envisions a scene of seven clocks on a mantelpiece.

Seeking stimulation to escape the monotony of the countryside, Bondel informs her father, Lord Caterham (also referred to as Lord Ketteman or Lord Cutler), that she is taking the Siamese cat into town. Lord Caterham expresses his preference for the quiet life and praises the attentiveness of Tredwell. Tredwell interrupts to inform Lord Caterham that the vicar wishes to discuss objections regarding the Boy Scouts.

While driving her Xavier on a sunny October day, Bondel—who is an experienced but fast driver—is forced to swerve when a man suddenly rushes onto the road. Although she believes she avoided a direct hit, the man is knocked down and lies motionless. Bondel discovers the man is a well-dressed young man with a mustache. Before dying, the man gasps a message: "Seven clocks... Tell... Jimmy... Sycamore..."

Believing she has killed him, a shaken Bondel drags the body into her car and drives to a nearby clinic. Dr. Castle treats her for shock with a drink and goes to examine the man. After a long wait, Dr. Castle returns with a severe and alert demeanor, requesting that Bondel explain exactly what happened during the accident.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondel recounts an incident to Dr. Castle, explaining that she believed she had nearly run over a man walking unsteadily near a roadside gate. However, Dr. Castle reveals that the man was not hit by the car but was actually shot with a rifle bullet, causing internal bleeding. Before dying, the man mentioned a "seven o'clock clock." Dr. Castle suggests the shooter may have been an accidental bystander or someone who did not realize they hit a person.

Bondel and Dr. Castle go to the police station, where an officer dismisses the shooting as local troublemakers practicing target shooting. Bondel identifies the deceased as Ronny DeVille from Albany, using a business card found on the body. This realization causes Bondel significant distress; she recognizes DeVille as a friend of Bill from the State Department and links his death to the previous death of Gary Wade. She also connects the "seven-faced clock" to a detail in Gary Wade's final letter to his sister.

Bondel returns home to inform her father, Lord Kittelam, of the situation. After a confused exchange, she asks him about the "seven-faced clock" and a man named Jimmy Sycamore. Lord Kittelam initially appears distracted but eventually recalls that George Lomax, a Cabinet Minister and the Queen's personal assistant for foreign affairs, received a threatening letter. Lomax claimed the threat originated from the "seven-faced clock" and intended to report the matter to Scotland Yard.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondell discusses the death of Gerry Wade with Lord Cuttle, learning that Lanlan Wade is not Gerry's biological sister. Although she shares the Wade surname, she is the child of the old Wade's second wife, who took the name after the old Wade insisted upon it. Bondell realizes that Gerry's letter to Lanlan was unusual and decides she must find Jimmy Sassiage and speak with Lorraine Wade, believing Lorraine may hold clues regarding a seven-faced clock and an ominous message Gerry left her.

The next morning, Bondell calls Bill to ask about Jimmy Sassiage. Bill describes Jimmy as a smart but idle man with a rosy face. During the call, Bill mentions Ronnie Devereux, Gerry's best friend, noting that he hasn't seen him since the weekend Gerry died. Bondell realizes Bill is unaware of Ronnie's death and suspects the police may have covered up the news since it was not reported in the papers. She decides not to tell Bill the truth over the phone but agrees to meet him for dinner the following night.

Bill provides Jimmy's address as 103 Jemming Street. Bondell travels there and is met by a polite, expressionless man who leads her to a living room. There, she encounters a petite girl in a black dress. Recognizing the girl's mourning clothes, Bondell asks if she is Lorraine Wade. Lorraine confirms her identity and mentions receiving a letter from Bondell regarding Gerry. When Bondell asks about Ronnie Devereux, Lorraine reveals she knows he is dead. After Bondell recounts the previous day's events, a terrified Lorraine concludes that Gerry Wade did not die naturally but was murdered.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lorena and Eileen Bondell visit Jimmy Cessie to discuss the suspicious deaths of Gerry Wade and Ronnie. Lorena notes that Gerry never used sleeping pills, a fact Ronnie also found suspicious. After being woken by his servant, Stevens, Jimmy learns that two young women are waiting to see him. The first visitor is Lorraine, who is accompanied by Bondell.

During their meeting, Lorraine reveals that Ronnie was shot and killed the previous day. Jimmy explains that on the day Gerry died, Ronnie had acted strangely and attempted to reveal information but claimed he had promised someone he would remain silent. Jimmy suspects Ronnie was secretly investigating Gerry's death and was killed to prevent him from uncovering the truth. The conversation centers on "the seven-faced clock," which Jimmy concludes is likely the headquarters of a secret organization.

Lorena presents a letter left by Gerry, which Bondell had discovered. Lorena recalls seeing a different letter intended for Gerry that featured an inscription of seven clocks and contained a list of names and dates. She notes that Gerry had jokingly remarked on the lack of imagination in British criminals compared to organizations like the Mafia. Jimmy deduces that Gerry wanted Lorena to forget about the matter to protect her from the danger posed by this organization. Jimmy warns the group that pursuing the investigation will put them all in danger.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy Sycamore, Loane, and Bondel discuss the murder of Gerry Wade and the suspicious death of Ronnie DeFleur. Loane, Gerry's only relative, expresses a determined motive to seek justice for her brother. Jimmy suggests they lack sufficient clues, noting they only have the phrase "seven clock" and do not know its location.

Bondel proposes a plan to investigate Gerry's death, theorizing that someone at the Chimney House poisoned him with chloral by dissolving it in his water glass. To narrow the suspect pool, Bondel suggests reviewing the staff list to identify recent hires, specifically mentioning a new butler named John. The group discusses various people present at the time, including Nancy, Helen, Socks Davenport, Gary Wade, Bill Everly, Sir Oswald, Lady Cutt, and Beattman, Sir Oswald's secretary.

The conversation shifts to the "seven clocks" mentioned in their clues. Jimmy recalls seeing only seven clocks on the mantelpiece in Gerry's room, rather than eight, and notes his discomfort with them. Bondel questions how the clocks were acquired, and Jimmy explains they were purchased collectively after Beattman suggested using alarms to wake Gerry, with Bill Everly suggesting they buy a dozen.

Jimmy concludes that Gerry likely discovered a secret organization similar to the Mafia, which led to his death and Ronnie's subsequent murder. The group's investigation is interrupted by Stevens, who serves them lunch. The discussion resumes when Bondel reveals that George Lomax, known as "Old Codfish," received a threatening letter mentioning "seven o'clock" regarding a party he is hosting the following week. Jimmy concludes that something is planned to occur at that party.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy Scegesh informs Lorel and Bondel of his discovery regarding Gerry Wade. He believes Gerry, who appears to be an unremarkable Foreign Office employee, is actually a high-ranking member of a secret intelligence agency. Jimmy notes that Gerry was not in Britain during the war and speaks German fluently, leading Jimmy to conclude that the Seven O'Clock Club is an international criminal organization. To investigate, Jimmy plans to attend an upcoming party hosted by George Lomax. Bondel suggests that Jimmy use Bill, George's assistant, to gain entry by posing as a wealthy playboy interested in politics. Bondel intends to meet Bill the following evening to secure a guest list. Jimmy insists that Lorel stay out of the operation to avoid suspicion, a directive she accepts with an obedience that Bondel finds suspicious.

Following the meeting, Bondel visits Scotland Yard to consult Inspector Bartlett, a detective who handles political cases. She inquires about secret organizations headquartered in the Seven Clocks area, but Bartlett claims the area has been rebuilt into an upper-class residential zone and no longer hosts such groups. Bondel attempts to probe further by mentioning the recent death of Ronny DeFleur and noting that DeFleur mentioned a "seven o'clock clock" before dying. She also brings up George Lomax’s recent visit regarding a threatening letter marked with the Seven Dials symbol. Bartlett remains uncooperative and dismissive, ultimately advising Bondel to stop investigating the matter.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Bartholomew (also referred to as Bardell or Batter) and Miss Bondell discuss the advantages of amateur investigation. Bondell questions the official account of Gerald Wade’s death, noting that while the inspector claims Wade overdosed on sleeping pills, Wade's sister maintains he never took them. Bartholomew provides Bondell with a list of secret organizations, including the Saint Sebastian Brotherhood, Wolf Hounds, Peace Fellows, Comrades Club, Oppressed Alliance, Sons of Moscow, Red Sign, Herring Society, and Decadent Alliance. He expresses concern that her involvement would cause trouble, but after Bondell argues that her amateur status allows her to act where professionals cannot, he offers a hint. He suggests that Bill Everly can tell her everything regarding the "seven-faced clock."

The following day, Bondell meets with Bill for dinner. Bill spends much of the meal gossiping about an American actress named Barbie Saint Moore, who uses a name from a "Famous People Dictionary" and was involved in a dispute within the musical play "Blinded by the Light." Bondell eventually steers the conversation toward Jimmy Sessage and his upcoming trip to the Double Dragon Church. Bill expresses concern that Jimmy might be manipulated by a politician referred to as "the old codfish," specifically mentioning Mrs. McCarta, an MP focused on welfare and children. Bill also mentions that an attractive "young Hungarian" woman will be attending the upcoming party.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bill provides Bondel with a list of individuals, including Lady MacCarty, Sir Stanley Digby (the aviation minister), his secretary Terence O'Roke, an inventor named Hel Eberhard, and Sir Oswald Kut and Lady Kut. Bondel questions Bill about whether these people are related to the seven-faced clock, but Bill feigns ignorance. 

Bondel asks about the Seven Dials Club, which Bill describes as a crowded, unrefined nightclub located in a former slum on Tottenham Court Road that serves fish and chips. Despite Bill's reluctance and his embarrassment over a past incident involving a fight over a lobster with Barbie Saint-Moore, Bondel insists they visit. They travel by taxi to 14 Hanstein Street. Upon arrival, Bondel notices a tall, golden-haired man with a shifty expression at the door. Inside the smoke-filled basement, they find a diverse crowd of foreigners, wealthy women, and sex workers. Upstairs, Bondel recognizes the gambling room guard as Alfred, a former second-class butler from the Chimney House who left a month prior to improve his situation. After observing the intense gamblers and spending time dancing and eating, Bondel decides to head home.

The following morning, Bondel visits her father, Lord Kettlemere, at the Chimney Cottage. He informs her that the county police chief, Melrose, will visit at noon the next day regarding the inquest for Ronnie Devereux, as Bondel found the body and must testify. Lord Kettlemere also mentions that George Lomax invited him to the Two-Footed Dragon Church the following week.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondel visits the Chimney House to speak with the housekeeper, Mrs. Howell. During their conversation, Bondel observes that the household staff includes two inefficient country maids and a competent housemaid who is Howell's niece. Bondel notes that Howell appears to bully Lady Cuttel and observes that the servants do not seem to like Sir Osbert Cutt. Bondel also inquires about the death of Mr. Gerry Wade, though Mrs. Howell provides no significant information.

Bondel then summons the butler, Tredwell, to ask about a former employee named Alfred, who left for London a month prior. Tredwell introduces the new footman, John, noting he was previously employed by Lord Montfort. Upon returning to the Chimney House, Bondel observes that John has a military bearing and an unusual head shape. While reflecting on the name "Bauer," Bondel suspects John has German rather than Swiss ancestry, noting his arrival coincided with the period leading up to Mr. Wade's death.

Bondel informs her father, Lord Keltram, that she is going to see her aunt, Lady Marchioness Ketteman. Lord Keltram warns her against visiting the formidable widow, but Bondel also asks him about Sir Osbert Cutt’s profession. He explains that Sir Osbert is in the steel business and owns large factories. 

As she travels to London, Bondel attempts to connect various elements: the steel industry, infant welfare, the inventor Mr. Hel Eberhard, and the Secretary of State for Aviation. She concludes that one of these interests must be a facade. Finally, Bondel meets with Lady Marchioness Ketteman, a large woman with a hooked nose and a beard, who expresses disapproval regarding the renting of the Chimney House.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Viscountess Kettelbach encourages Bondel to pursue a career in politics, suggesting she meet Mrs. McCarta, an influential woman. Bondel expresses interest but notes that her father did not invite her to George Lomax's upcoming house party. The Viscountess promises to speak with Lomax to ensure Bondel's attendance. Afterward, Bondel receives political research materials from the Viscountess's secretary and contacts Jimmy Sexton. Jimmy reveals he is researching the Santa Fe border dispute and mentions he is studying a note sent from Bolivia to Britain. The two agree to keep their research secret from Lorena Wade to protect her from danger. While Bondel prepares for an autopsy the following day, Jimmy plans to take Lorena to dinner.

Bondel then travels to the Seven Dials Club at 14 Hanstein Street, wearing borrowed clothes to disguise herself. Her goal is to confront her former butler, Alfred. She finds Alfred exiting the club and insists on speaking with him inside. Bondel accuses him of illegal activities and asks how much he was paid to leave Chimney House. Alfred admits that a Russian gentleman named Mr. Mosgrove, who runs the club, gave him £100 and instructed him to manage the club to provide it with more "class." Alfred explains that Mosgrove also arranged for a replacement servant at Chimney House. Bondel warns Alfred about the risk of forced labor and mentions she visited Scotland Yard. She promises to protect him if he agrees to help her with her investigation.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondel and Alfred explore a location, eventually discovering a secret, inconspicuous door in the corner of a gambling room. Alfred explains that the door serves as an escape route to a staircase leading to the next street, disguised externally as a closet. After trying several keys, Bondel successfully opens the door to find a small, dimly lit room containing a long table with seven chairs, a fireplace, and two built-in closets. Bondel concludes that the room is a meeting place for a secret organization, noting the soundproofing velvet on the door frame and the strategic layout.

Alfred mentions that Mr. Mosgolovsky had him clean the room that morning. Seeking a place to hide, Bondel identifies a wardrobe filled with glassware as a potential hiding spot. She instructs a reluctant Alfred to move the items to a pantry so she can climb inside. After Alfred retrieves a tray and tools, Bondel uses a screwdriver to drill a small, inconspicuous hole in the wardrobe door at eye level. She instructs Alfred to lock her inside, take the key, and return the room's keys to their proper places. She tells him to claim he lost the wardrobe key if Mr. Mosgolovsky asks.

Bondel waits in the cramped closet for several hours. Around six in the morning, she hears the door unlock. A tall, broad-shouldered man with a black beard enters; Bondel identifies him as the club owner, Mr. Moskrovsky. After he checks his watch, he puts on a unique mask that hangs like a curtain over his face, shaped like a clock face with a pointer indicating six o'clock.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While hiding in a closet, Bondell observes a secret meeting of individuals wearing hourglass masks. The participants include a bearded Russian named Mosgolovsky, who acts as the host; an American with an Irish lilt identified as "four o'clock"; an elegant, tall man identified as "five o'clock"; and a beautiful woman with a Slavic appearance. A person identified as "three o'clock," who possesses a well-bred British accent, also attends. The group discusses the absence of "two o'clock" and the mysterious "seven o'clock," the leader who called the meeting but has never been seen.

The members express concern regarding "danger" and the possibility of people discovering their location and the "chimney house." Mosgolovsky emphasizes the need to silence suspicious individuals. The meeting shifts to business regarding a "two-footed dragon church" and a guest list that includes figures such as Sir Stanley Digby and Countess Anna Razky. They discuss the immense commercial and international value of an invention by Herr Eberhard.

The conversation reveals that a letter from Gerry Wade mentioning their organization was discovered by Miss Irene Brent, a failure attributed to someone named Bourne. Additionally, they confirm that the death of Ronnie De Vere has been successfully covered up as an accident involving a local boy. After the meeting concludes with a salute to "Anna," the members depart through a hidden door. Two hours later, Alfred arrives to release a pale and anxious Bondell.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After returning home at 6:00 am, Bondel wakes at 9:30 am and calls Jimmy Sexton, who is preparing for an autopsy. Bondel requests that Jimmy pick her up and drop her at Chimney House to meet the county police chief. During their drive, Bondel recounts a long and mysterious series of events involving a foreign adventurer, an international gang, and a mysterious "seven o'clock" meeting. 

Jimmy realizes the situation mirrors tropes from fiction but acknowledges its reality. They discuss the diverse group of people involved, including a Russian, an American, a British man, and a mysterious woman. Bondel identifies a German presence, noting that Bauer, the butler, was involved in an attempt to obtain a report regarding Chimney House, which Jimmy suspects is related to the death of Gerry Wade.

The conversation shifts to Hel Eberhard, a German inventor. Jimmy recalls that Eberhard applied for a patent on a steel technology that could revolutionize aviation by making objects stronger and lighter. Bondel explains that Eberhard is planning to bring his formula to an unofficial meeting at the Double Dragon Church, which includes Sir Oswald Kutt and the aviation minister. The "Seven-Faced Clock" group intends to steal this formula, which a Russian claimed is worth millions.

Bondel also reveals that a man named Ronnie's final words were, "Seven-Faced Clock, tell Jimmy Sessage." Jimmy concludes that a messenger named Paul was likely responsible for Mr. Grey's death. As they arrive at Chimney House, they meet Major Melrose and proceed to an inquest. Bondel and the doctor testify, and the death is ruled an accident caused by someone playing with a gun.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following an inquest, Major Melrose drives Bondell back to the Chimney House while Jimmy returns to London. Disturbed by Bondell's account, Jimmy calls Lorna to inform her that the inquest ruled an incident an accident, though he suspects a cover-up. He warns her to be careful, prompting her to express concern for his own safety. Jimmy then instructs his associate, Stevens, to procure an automatic handgun with a blued barrel.

On Friday afternoon, Bondell attends a gala at the Two-Footed Dragon Church. George Lomax greets her warmly, noting her interest in politics. Jimmy meets Bondell there, appearing more serious than usual, and whispers to her that he has informed Bill about their "little scheme." Bondell expresses frustration with Bill's perceived lack of intelligence, though Jimmy defends him, noting Bill's physical strength.

During the event, George introduces Bondell to various guests, including Sir Stanley Digby, Mr. O'Roke, Mr. Osborne, Lady Cutt, Mr. Bettman, and the beautiful Countess Razky. Lady Cutt discusses a recent car accident with Jimmy, while Jimmy privately mocks the "gorilla" (referring to a guest) who is enchanted by the Countess. Sir Oswald Kuter discusses property with Bondell, expressing interest in the Chimney House, which leaves her feeling indignant. 

After changing her clothes, Bondell encounters a bulky man dressed like a butler. She identifies him as Detective Inspector Bartholomew, who has arrived in disguise to protect Mr. Lomax following a threatening letter. Bondell finds his disguise unconvincing, but the Inspector remains undeterred.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Bondell and Butler discuss the need for discretion to avoid alerting potential thieves to their presence. Meanwhile, George informs Bondell that Mrs. McCarta cannot attend due to her child having mumps. George describes the Countess, a wealthy Hungarian leader of the Young Hungarian Party dedicated to public service and reducing infant mortality, as a special guest. Bondell meets Hel Eberhard, a nervous German inventor, before Bill Everly arrives, exhausted from entertaining the Countess. Jimmy Sassage reveals that the Countess intends to have Bill take her to an interesting old location after tea. Bondell expresses concern regarding Bill's ability to handle the Countess.

Following dinner, the ladies gather in the drawing room of the Double-Footed Dragon Church, which lacks central heating. The Countess, Lady Kut, and Bondell struggle with the cold. During the conversation, the ladies discuss the mumps, and Lady Bendl provides a vivid, exaggerated account of the misery in post-war Budapest. The Countess uses these stories to emphasize her belief that Hungary lacks organization rather than money. Lady Kut reflects on her husband Sir Oswald’s success, which she attributes to his meticulous and methodical nature. The passage concludes with Lady Kut asking Bondell about the head gardener, McDonald, noting with envy that McDonald follows Bondell's orders.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy Cessie lures Bondel away from the Countess to the library, where Bill is waiting. Bill expresses anxiety regarding Bondel’s involvement in the "seven-faced clock club" matter, fearing someone might get hurt, citing the fate of Ronnie. Bondel remains determined to stay involved. 

The group discusses a mysterious formula held by either Herr Eberhard or Sir Oswald. Bill suggests the formula will be handed to the Air Minister tonight to eventually fall into the hands of O'Roke, and that Sir Stanley Digby will return to town tomorrow. Jimmy notes that if Sir Stanley intends to take the formula, something significant must occur tonight. Jimmy also mentions meeting a man in the hall, wondering if it was Watson or Lestrade, though Bill believes it was Watson.

To monitor the situation, Jimmy and Bill decide to take turns on watch. They toss a coin to decide; it lands on tails, meaning Jimmy takes the first watch and will wake Bill at three o'clock. Bondel expresses suspicion toward the Countess, but Bill defends her, citing a guarantee from the Hungarian embassy. Bondel eventually retires to bed.

Jimmy reveals he has a powerful blued-steel automatic pistol, which he obtained through his servant, Stevens. After Bill repeatedly says goodnight and departs, Jimmy is left alone to begin his watch.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy Sessage keeps watch at a junction in the west corridor near the rooms of Sir Stanley Diggie and Terence O'Rourke. Sitting in an oak cabinet's shadow with a Leopold automatic pistol, Jimmy experiences anxiety and dark thoughts regarding the deaths of Ronnie DeVere and Gerry Wade. He wonders about the motives behind the seven ticking clocks on the mantelpiece and questions whether Bondel Brent's stories about the Seven Clocks Club are fabricated. Around two o'clock, Jimmy hears creaking sounds from below. He descends to the library, finds it empty, but discovers an unlocked middle window facing the terrace. He locks the library door, pockets the key, and waits by the open window.

Meanwhile, Bondel Brent, seeking to supplement the efforts of Bill and Jimmy, decides to investigate the exterior of the house. She dresses in riding pants, rubber-soled shoes, and a dark sweater to facilitate climbing. At 1:30, she climbs down the ivy from her window and moves along the terrace toward the southern end. While navigating the shadows, she is intercepted by Inspector Batter. Batter, who is tall and well-built, claims he is checking for unauthorized visitors. He questions Bondel's presence and identifies that she has climbed out of a window. Despite Bondel's resentment that his conspicuous presence might interfere with her plans, Batter remains on the terrace.

title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
